# Artificial intelligence in India

- [x] 3 languages 

*   [العربية](https://ar.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%B0%D9%83%D8%A7%D8%A1_%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%A7%D8%B5%D8%B7%D9%86%D8%A7%D8%B9%D9%8A_%D9%81%D9%8A_%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%87%D9%86%D8%AF "الذكاء الاصطناعي في الهند – Arabic")
*   [বাংলা](https://bn.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E0%A6%AD%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%87_%E0%A6%95%E0%A7%83%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%AE_%E0%A6%AC%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%A6%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A7%E0%A6%BF%E0%A6%AE%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%BE "ভারতে কৃত্রিম বুদ্ধিমত্তা – Bangla")
*   [हिन्दी](https://hi.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E0%A4%AD%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%A4_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A5%87%E0%A4%82_%E0%A4%95%E0%A5%83%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%AE_%E0%A4%AC%E0%A5%81%E0%A4%A6%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%A7%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%A4%E0%A4%BE "भारत में कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्ता – Hindi")

Appearance

move to sidebar hide

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **[artificial intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence "Artificial intelligence")** (AI) market in India is projected to reach $8 billion by 2025, growing at 40% [CAGR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CAGR "CAGR") from 2020 to 2025.[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-trade-1) This growth is part of the broader [AI boom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AI_boom "AI boom"), a global period of rapid technological advancements with India being pioneer starting in the early 2010s with NLP based Chatbots from [Haptik](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haptik "Haptik"),[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-2) Corover.ai, [Niki.ai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niki.ai "Niki.ai") and then gaining prominence in the early 2020s based on [reinforcement learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reinforcement_learning "Reinforcement learning"), marked by breakthroughs such as [generative AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Generative_artificial_intelligence "Generative artificial intelligence") models from [Krutrim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kruti "Kruti"), Sarvam, CoRover, [OpenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAI "OpenAI") and [Alphafold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AlphaFold "AlphaFold") by [Google DeepMind](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Google_DeepMind "Google DeepMind"). In India, the development of AI has been similarly transformative, with [applications in healthcare](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_healthcare "Artificial intelligence in healthcare"), finance, and education, bolstered by government initiatives like [NITI Aayog](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NITI_Aayog "NITI Aayog")'s 2018[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-3) National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence. Institutions such as the [Indian Statistical Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Statistical_Institute "Indian Statistical Institute") and the [Indian Institute of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science "Indian Institute of Science") published breakthrough AI research papers and patents.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-4)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-5)[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-6)

India's transformation to AI is primarily being driven by [startups](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Startup_India "Startup India") and government initiatives & policies like [Digital India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_India "Digital India"). By fostering technological trust through [digital public infrastructure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_public_infrastructure "Digital public infrastructure"), India is tackling socioeconomic issues by taking a bottom-up approach to AI. [NASSCOM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASSCOM "NASSCOM") and [Boston Consulting Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boston_Consulting_Group "Boston Consulting Group") estimate that by 2027, India's AI services might be valued at $17 billion.[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-7) According to 2025 Technology and Innovation Report, by [UN Trade and Development](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UN_Trade_and_Development "UN Trade and Development"), India ranks 10th globally for private sector investments in AI.[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-8) According to [Mary Meeker](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mary_Meeker "Mary Meeker"), India has emerged as a key market for AI platforms, accounting for the largest share of [ChatGPT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ChatGPT "ChatGPT")'s mobile app users and having the third-largest user base for [DeepSeek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DeepSeek_(chatbot) "DeepSeek (chatbot)") in 2025.[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-9)[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-10)

While AI presents significant opportunities for economic growth and social development in India, challenges such as data privacy concerns, skill shortages, and ethical considerations need to be addressed for responsible AI deployment.[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-carnegie-11) The growth of AI in India has also led to an increase in the number of [cyberattacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyberattack "Cyberattack") that use AI to target organizations.[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-12)

## History

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Early days (1960s-1980s)

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early days (1960s-1980s)")]

The [TIFRAC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TIFRAC "TIFRAC") (Tata Institute of Fundamental Research Automatic Calculator) was designed and developed by a team led by [Rangaswamy Narasimhan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rangaswamy_Narasimhan "Rangaswamy Narasimhan") between 1954 and 1960. He worked on [pattern recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pattern_recognition "Pattern recognition") from 1961 to 1964 at the [University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Illinois_Urbana-Champaign "University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign")'s Digital Computer Laboratory. In order to conduct research on database technology, [computer networking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_networking "Computer networking"), [computer graphics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_graphics "Computer graphics"), and [systems software](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/System_software "System software"), he and [M. G. K. Menon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M._G._K._Menon "M. G. K. Menon") founded the National Centre for Software Development and Computing Techniques.[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-13) In 1965, he established the [Computer Society of India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_Society_of_India "Computer Society of India") and supervised the initial research work on AI at [Tata Institute of Fundamental Research](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tata_Institute_of_Fundamental_Research "Tata Institute of Fundamental Research"). Jagdish Lal launched the first computer science program in 1976 at [Motilal Nehru Regional Engineering College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Motilal_Nehru_Regional_Engineering_College "Motilal Nehru Regional Engineering College"). [H. K. Kesavan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H._K._Kesavan "H. K. Kesavan") from the [University of Waterloo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Waterloo "University of Waterloo") and [Vaidyeswaran Rajaraman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vaidyeswaran_Rajaraman "Vaidyeswaran Rajaraman") from the [University of Wisconsin–Madison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin%E2%80%93Madison "University of Wisconsin–Madison") joined the [IIT Kanpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kanpur "IIT Kanpur") Electrical Engineering Department in 1963–1964 as Assistant Professor and Head of Department, respectively. H.N. Mahabala, who was employed at [Bendix Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bendix_Corporation "Bendix Corporation")'s Computer Division, joined the department in 1965. He previously worked with [Marvin Minsky](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marvin_Minsky "Marvin Minsky"). The IIT Kanpur Computer Center was led by H. K. Kesavan, with Vaidyeswaran Rajaraman serving as his deputy. Kesavan informally permitted Rajaraman and Mahabala to introduce artificial intelligence into computer science classes. The computer science program was approved by IIT Kanpur in 1971 and split out from the electrical engineering department. In 1973, an [IBM System/370 Model 155](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IBM_System/370_Model_155 "IBM System/370 Model 155") was installed at [IIT Madras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras"). [John McCarthy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_McCarthy_(computer_scientist) "John McCarthy (computer scientist)"), head of the Artificial Intelligence Laboratory at [Stanford University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stanford_University "Stanford University") visited IIT Kanpur in 1971. He donated [PDP-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDP-1 "PDP-1") with a [time-sharing operating system](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time-sharing_operating_system "Time-sharing operating system"). During the 1970s, the balance of payments deficit in India restricted import of computers. The Department of Computer Science and Automation at the [Indian Institute of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science "Indian Institute of Science") established in 1969, played an important role in nurturing the development of data science and artificial intelligence in India. First course on AI was introduced in 1970's by G. Krishna. [B. L. Deekshatulu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B._L._Deekshatulu "B. L. Deekshatulu") introduced the first course on pattern recognition in early 1970s.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:0-14)[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-15)[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-16)[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:2-17)[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:3-18)

### Foundation phase

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Foundation phase")]

#### 1980s

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 1980s")]

In the 1980s, the [Indian Statistical Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Statistical_Institute "Indian Statistical Institute")'s **Optical Character Recognition Project** was one of the country's first attempts at studying artificial intelligence and machine learning.OCR technology has benefited greatly from the work of ISI's Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Unit, which is headed by [Bidyut Baran Chaudhuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bidyut_Baran_Chaudhuri "Bidyut Baran Chaudhuri"). He also contributed in the development of [computer vision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_vision "Computer vision") and [digital image processing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_image_processing "Digital image processing").[[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-ieee2-19)[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-20)[[21]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-21) As part of the **Indian Fifth Generation Computer Systems Research Programme**, the [Department of Electronics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Electronics_%26_IT "Department of Electronics & IT"), with support from the [United Nations Development Programme](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Development_Programme "United Nations Development Programme"), initiated the **Knowledge Based Computer Systems Project** in 1986, marking the beginning of India's first major AI research program. [Prime Minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_India "Prime Minister of India")[Rajiv Gandhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajiv_Gandhi "Rajiv Gandhi") requested that the Department of Electronics and [IISc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IISc "IISc") to initiate the **Parallel Processing Project** in 1986–1987. The [Center for Development of Advanced Computing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centre_for_Development_of_Advanced_Computing "Centre for Development of Advanced Computing") eventually joined those efforts. IIT Madras was selected to develop system diagnosis, [ISI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Statistical_Institute "Indian Statistical Institute") for image processing, [National Centre for Software Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Centre_for_Software_Technology "National Centre for Software Technology") for [natural language processing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_language_processing "Natural language processing") and [TIFR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TIFR "TIFR") for [speech processing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speech_processing "Speech processing").[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:2-17)[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:0-14)[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:3-18)[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-22)[[23]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-23)

In 1987, the proposal of [N. Seshagiri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/N._Seshagiri "N. Seshagiri"), Director General of the [National Informatics Centre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Informatics_Centre "National Informatics Centre") for the prototype development of supercomputer was cleared.[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-24)[[25]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-25) Negotiations for a [Cray supercomputer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cray_supercomputer "Cray supercomputer") were underway between the [Reagan administration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reagan_administration "Reagan administration") and the [Rajiv Gandhi government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_Rajiv_Gandhi_ministry "Second Rajiv Gandhi ministry"). US Defense Secretaries [Frank Carlucci](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frank_Carlucci "Frank Carlucci") and [Caspar Weinberger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caspar_Weinberger "Caspar Weinberger") visited [New Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Delhi "New Delhi") after the US approved the transfer in 1988. The sale of a lower-end XMP-14 supercomputer was permitted in lieu of the [Cray](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cray "Cray") XMP-24 supercomputer due to security concerns. The Center for Development of Advanced Computing was formally established in March 1988 by the [Ministry of Communications and Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Communications_and_Information_Technology_(India) "Ministry of Communications and Information Technology (India)") (previously the Ministry of IT) within the [Department of Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Information_Technology "Department of Information Technology") (formerly the Department of Electronics) in response to a recommendation made to the Prime Minister by the Scientific Advisory Council. The **National Initiative in Supercomputing**, which produced the [PARAM series](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PARAM "PARAM"), was led by [Vijay P. Bhatkar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vijay_P._Bhatkar "Vijay P. Bhatkar"). For the first ten years, supercomputing and Indian language computing were the two main focus areas. [C-DAC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C-DAC "C-DAC") has expanded its operations in order to meet the needs in a number of domains, including network and internet software, [real-time systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Real-time_systems "Real-time systems"), artificial intelligence, and [NLP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_language_processing "Natural language processing").[[26]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-26)[[27]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-27)[[28]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-28)[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:1-29)[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-30)[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-31)

Under the direction of Professor KV Ramakrishnamacharyulu from [National Sanskrit University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Sanskrit_University "National Sanskrit University") and Professor Rajeev Sangal from the [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad"), the Akshar Bharati Research Group was established in 1984 with support from IIT Kanpur and the [University of Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Hyderabad "University of Hyderabad") for computational processing of [Indian languages](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_languages_(India) "Indian languages (India)"). They focused on [computational linguistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computational_linguistics "Computational linguistics"), NLP with ontological database systems, and Indian language/translation theories with linguistic tradition.[[32]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-32)[[33]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-33)[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-34)[[35]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-35)

#### 1990s

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 1990s")]

From IIT Kanpur, Mohan Tambe joined [C-DAC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C-DAC "C-DAC") in the 1990s to work on Graphics and Intelligence based Script Technology (GIST), which addressed the challenge of adapting personal computer software based on [Latin script](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Latin_script "Latin script") to [Devanagiri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devanagari_script "Devanagari script") and a number of other Indian language scripts. He was previously working on the **Machine Translation for Indian languages Project**. Within C-DAC, he established the GIST group. The technology was expanded to encompass [NLP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_language_processing "Natural language processing"), artificial intelligence-based machine-aided language learning and translation, multimedia and multilingual computing solutions, and more. GIST resulted in the creation of G-CLASS (GIST cross language search plug-ins suite), a cross-language search engine. The Applied Artificial Intelligence Group at C-DAC has developed some basic and novel applications in the field of NLP, including [machine translation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_translation "Machine translation"), information extraction/retrieval, [automatic summarization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Automatic_summarization "Automatic summarization"), [speech recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speech_recognition "Speech recognition"), [text-to-speech synthesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Text-to-speech_synthesizer "Text-to-speech synthesizer"), intelligent language teaching, and natural language-based document management with Decision Support Systems. These applications are the result of the foundation laid by previous language technology activities. Software firms in the Indian private sector began looking into AI applications, mostly in the area of [business process automation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Business_process_automation "Business process automation").[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:2-17)[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:0-14)[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:1-29)[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-36)

In order to allow machines to read, comprehend, and interpret [human languages](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_languages "Human languages"), the Language Technologies Research Center was founded in October 1999 at the [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad"). It focused on the advancements in [semantic parsing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semantic_parsing "Semantic parsing"), [information extraction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Information_extraction "Information extraction"), [natural language generation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_language_generation "Natural language generation"), [sentiment analysis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sentiment_analysis "Sentiment analysis"), and [dialogue systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dialogue_system "Dialogue system").[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-37)

Some of the early AI research in India was driven by societal needs.[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:2-17)[[38]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-38)[[39]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-39)[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-40) For example;

*   Eklavya, a knowledge-based program created by [IIT Madas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras"), helped [community health workers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Community_health_worker "Community health worker") deal with [toddler](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toddler "Toddler") illness symptoms by generating systematic case histories, offering basic treatment advice, or indicating when a [referral](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Referral_(medicine) "Referral (medicine)") was necessary.
*   The [National Centre for Software Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Centre_for_Software_Technology "National Centre for Software Technology") (NCST) created the Vidya language teaching system, which enhances educational quality through the use of speech-vision processing, [ML](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning "Machine learning"), and NLP.
*   [C-DAC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C-DAC "C-DAC") created the Sarani flight-scheduling expert system.
*   [TIFR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TIFR "TIFR") and the [Central Electronics Engineering Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Electronics_Engineering_Research_Institute "Central Electronics Engineering Research Institute") created a [formant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Formant_Synthesis "Formant Synthesis")-based speech synthesis system for the [Indian Railways](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Railways "Indian Railways").
*   [IISc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IISc "IISc") and [ISRO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISRO "ISRO") built an image processing facility that uses AI and computer vision.

### Growth phase

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Growth phase")]

Around 2003, [language technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Language_technology "Language technology"), [computer vision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_vision "Computer vision"), and [data science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_science "Data science") research groups were established at the [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad"). With an emphasis on applied and translational research, the institute has the largest group of AI and machine learning researchers in India as of 2023. They also work on blockchain and [quantum computing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing "Quantum computing"). In partnership with [Intel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intel "Intel"), they created the Indian Driving Dataset, which contains the largest amount of road data for unstructured driving situations worldwide.[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-41)

The [Government of Karnataka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Karnataka "Government of Karnataka") established the Machine Intelligence & Robotics CoE (MINRO) at the [International Institute of Information Technology, Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Bengaluru "International Institute of Information Technology, Bengaluru") in 2018 with the goal of creating frameworks and regulations for ethical and responsible AI technologies.[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-42)[[43]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-43) It researches on [human-machine interface](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human-machine_interface "Human-machine interface"), [industrial robotics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industrial_robot "Industrial robot") and [automation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Automation "Automation"), data science and analysis, pattern recognition, [machine intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_intelligence "Machine intelligence"), and AI systems.[[44]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-44)

On 7 September 2018, [NITI Aayog](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NITI_Aayog "NITI Aayog"), [Intel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intel "Intel"), and [TIFR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TIFR "TIFR") announced their intention to establish [International Center for Transformative Artificial Intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=International_Center_for_Transformative_Artificial_Intelligence&action=edit&redlink=1 "International Center for Transformative Artificial Intelligence (page does not exist)") (ICTAI) in [Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengaluru "Bengaluru") to support applied research. Under the National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence, it will create and implement AI-led application-based research projects on AI foundational frameworks, tools, and assets, such as curated datasets and distinctive AI algorithms in smart mobility, healthcare, and agriculture.[[45]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-45)[[46]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-46) On 16 November 2018, the [Government of Maharastra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Maharashtra "Government of Maharashtra") signed [MoU](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MoU "MoU") with the NITI Aayog and [Wadhwani Institute for Artificial Intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wadhwani_Institute_for_Artificial_Intelligence "Wadhwani Institute for Artificial Intelligence") to launch ICTAI for [rural healthcare](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rural_health_care_in_India "Rural health care in India") in collaboration with [PATH](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PATH_(global_health_organization) "PATH (global health organization)"), Lords Education and Health Society, Wadhwani Initiative for Sustainable Healthcare, [IIT Madras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras"), [Sir Ratan Tata Trust](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sir_Ratan_Tata_Trust "Sir Ratan Tata Trust"), and Stanford Center for Population Health Sciences. It will prioritize study, diagnosis, and treatment of illnesses such as [tuberculosis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuberculosis_in_India "Tuberculosis in India") and [cancer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cancer "Cancer").[[47]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-47)[[48]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-48)

NITI Aayog signed partnership agreement with [Microsoft](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft") in 2018 to help expedite the use of AI for the development and adoption of local language computing, and to create [farm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Farm "Farm") advice services. Microsoft-NITI Aayog Problem to Solution Incubation Test Bed will be established. Microsoft will also help develop AI-assisted models for [diabetic retinopathy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diabetic_retinopathy "Diabetic retinopathy") screening.[[49]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-49)[[50]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-50)

NITI Aayog drafted a proposal in 2019 to establish an institutional framework for AI. A cabinet note has been issued proposing to allocate ₹7,500 crore initially over three years for the establishment of 20 AI adaption centers, five core research institutes, and a cloud computing platform named AIRAWAT.[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-51)[[52]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-52)

### Acceleration phase

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Acceleration phase")]

[INDIAai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INDIAai "INDIAai"), a collaborative initiative of the National E-Government Division and [NASSCOM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASSCOM "NASSCOM") for AI-related advancements, was introduced by [Ravi Shankar Prasad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ravi_Shankar_Prasad "Ravi Shankar Prasad") on 30 May 2020. In partnership with Intel and the [Ministry of Education](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Education_(India) "Ministry of Education (India)"), the **Responsible AI for Youth Program** was also introduced to foster the development of AI-related skills.[[53]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-53) The Yardi School of Artificial Intelligence was founded in September 2020 by [IIT Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Delhi "IIT Delhi") to advance research in AI, ML, and data science in the fields of healthcare, [materials science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Materials_science "Materials science"), robotics, [industry 4.0](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industry_4.0 "Industry 4.0"), [weather prediction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Weather_prediction "Weather prediction"), and transportation.[[54]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-54) $10 million was contributed by [Yardi Systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yardi_Systems "Yardi Systems")' Anant Yardi in 2021 for education, infrastructure and research related activities.[[55]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-55) Sharad Kumar Saraf and Sudarshan Kumar Saraf, the founders of Technocraft Industries, donated ₹15 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") to establish the Technocraft Centre for Applied Artificial Intelligence at [IIT Bombay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Bombay "IIT Bombay") in March 2021.[[56]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-56)[[57]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-57) In 2021, the Mehta Family Foundation and [IIT Guwahati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Guwahati "IIT Guwahati") signed an [MoU](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MoU "MoU") to establish the Mehta Family School of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence.[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-58)

According to [Stanford University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stanford_University "Stanford University")'s annual AI Index report, India ranked fifth globally in 2022 in terms of investments received by businesses offering AI products and services.[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-59) In 2022, Indian software professionals made the most global contribution to nearly 24% of [GitHub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GitHub "GitHub") projects pertaining to AI.[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-60)

The Centre for Machine Intelligence and Data Science was officially opened by IIT Bombay on 28 April 2023.[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-61)[[62]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-62) The [National Research Foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=National_Research_Foundation_(India)&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Research Foundation (India) (page does not exist)") was created to advance research in a variety of fields, including AI with a budget of ₹50,000 crore for five years.[[63]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-63)[[64]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-64)

The [Union Cabinet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Union_Cabinet "Union Cabinet") approved an extension of the [Digital India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_India "Digital India") program in 2023, allocating ₹14,903 crore starting FY2021-22 to FY2025-26 for the addition of nine new supercomputers under the National Super Computer Mission. All 22 of the Schedule 8 languages will be available for [Bhashini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhashini "Bhashini"). There will be the establishment of three AI Centers of Excellence focused on sustainable cities, agriculture, and health.[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-65)[[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-66)[Prime Minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_India "Prime Minister of India")[Narendra Modi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Narendra_Modi "Narendra Modi") addressed international concerns about the negative use of artificial intelligence during a virtual [G20](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/G20 "G20") conference hosted by India in November 2023.[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-67)

To assist the Indian government and policymakers on data science and AI-related policy issues, [Sunil Wadhwani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sunil_Wadhwani "Sunil Wadhwani") gave ₹110 crore to [IIT Madras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras") in 2024 for Wadhwani School of Data Science and AI. The institute will combine fundamental and applied research in systems biology, manufacturing, energy and the environment, healthcare, agriculture, smart cities and transportation, financial analytics, and defense.[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-68)

India invested [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")10,371.92 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$1.2 billion) on **IndiaAI Mission**.[[69]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-69)[[70]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-70) With a budget of ₹990 crore, [Minister for Education](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Education_(India) "Ministry of Education (India)")[Dharmendra Pradhan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dharmendra_Pradhan "Dharmendra Pradhan") announced on 15 October 2024, the creation of three AI Centers of Excellence in [New Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Delhi "New Delhi"): one for agricultural (under [IIT Ropar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Ropar "IIT Ropar")), one for sustainable cities (under [IIT Kanpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kanpur "IIT Kanpur")), and one for healthcare (under [AIIMS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AIIMS "AIIMS") and [IIT Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Delhi "IIT Delhi")).[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-71) The Center for Generative AI, Shrijan at [IIT Jodhpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Jodhpur "IIT Jodhpur") was established on 25 October 2024 by [Meta Platforms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meta_Platforms "Meta Platforms") and IndiaAI to support long-term sustainability of [Foundation Models](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_model "Foundation model") and [GenAI research](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GenAI "GenAI") in healthcare, education, agriculture, smart cities, smart mobility, sustainability, financial inclusion, and social inclusion. Additionally, the **YuvAI initiative for Skilling and Capacity Building** was launched in collaboration with the [All India Council for Technical Education](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/All_India_Council_for_Technical_Education "All India Council for Technical Education") to advance [open-source AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_source_ai "Open source ai"). Meta has pledged to donate up to ₹750 [lakhs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lakh "Lakh") over a three-year period. [Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gati_Shakti_Vishwavidyalaya "Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya"), the [Institute of Human Behavior and Allied Sciences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Institute_of_Human_Behaviour_and_Allied_Sciences "Institute of Human Behaviour and Allied Sciences"), the [Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postgraduate_Institute_of_Medical_Education_and_Research "Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research"), and the [All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Jodhpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/All_India_Institute_of_Medical_Sciences,_Jodhpur "All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Jodhpur"), will work together with Shrijan.[[72]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-72)[[73]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-73) In November 2024, the [Karnataka government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karnataka_government "Karnataka government") approved a ₹28 crore investment to construct a Center of Excellence in Artificial Intelligence in [Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengaluru "Bengaluru").[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-74)

From 2025 to 2027, [Microsoft](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft") to invest [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")25,000 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$3.0 billion) on [cloud](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cloud_computing "Cloud computing") and AI infrastructure.[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:16-75) The construction of a 3 [GW](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gigawatt "Gigawatt")[data center](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_center "Data center") for AI services in [Jamnagar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jamnagar "Jamnagar") was announced by [Reliance Industries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reliance_Industries "Reliance Industries") in 2025.[[76]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-76) On 12 June 2025, India's first locally developed, [multilingual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multilingualism "Multilingualism")[agentic AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agentic_AI "Agentic AI"), [Kruti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kruti "Kruti"), was introduced by Ola Krutrim.[[77]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:62-77)

## National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems")]

The mission which has a five-year budget of ₹3,660 crore, was authorized by the Union Cabinet in December 2018 under the [Department of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)"). A technological vertical in AI and ML, [IoT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IoT "IoT"), [data bank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_bank "Data bank") and [DaaS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DaaS "DaaS"), [data analysis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_analysis "Data analysis"), autonomous systems and robotics, [cyber security](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyber_security "Cyber security"), and [quantum engineering](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_engineering "Quantum engineering") has been assigned to each of the 25 technological innovation hubs that have been formed.[[78]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-78)

To translate academic research on AI at the proof-of-concept stage into commercially viable goods and services, IIT Kharagpur established the AI4ICPS Innovation Hub Foundation in 2020. Under the National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS), the Department of Science and Technology awarded it a grant of ₹170 crore.[[79]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-79)[[80]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-80)

With a [seed money](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seed_money "Seed money") of ₹230 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") from the [Department of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)") and the [Government of Karnataka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Karnataka "Government of Karnataka"), an Artificial Intelligence and Robotics Technology Park was established at the [Indian Institute of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science "Indian Institute of Science") as not-for-profit foundation in November 2020 for mission-mode research and development projects in cyber-security, healthcare, education, mobility, infrastructure, agriculture, and retail.[[81]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-81)[[82]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-82)

TiHAN at [IIT Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad") hosted India's first Autonomous Navigations Testbed Facility (Aerial & Terrestrial) in 2022, which was opened by [Jitendra Singh Rana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jitendra_Singh_Rana "Jitendra Singh Rana") from [Ministry of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Ministry of Science and Technology (India)") to develop next-generation autonomous navigation technology.[[83]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-83)[[84]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-84)

To develop spectral and energy-efficient wireless communications technology for [5G](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/5G "5G") and [5G-Advanced](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/5G-Advanced "5G-Advanced"), Kiran Kumar Kuchi of IIT Hyderabad and IIITB Comet Foundation in 2024 developed an O-RAN base station solution that triples capacity and improves cell coverage when compared to [4G](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/4G "4G") networks.[[85]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-85)

**Technology Innovation Hubs**[[86]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-86)

| Name | Location | Area of research |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AI4ICPS I-Hub Foundation | [IIT Kharagpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kharagpur "IIT Kharagpur") | Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning |
| TIH Foundation for IoT and IoE | [IIT Bombay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Bombay "IIT Bombay") | [Internet of things](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_of_things "Internet of things")& Internet of everything |
| IIIT-H Data I-Hub Foundation | [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad") | Data Banks & Data Services, Data Analysis |
| I-HUB for Robotics and Autonomous Systems Innovation Foundation | [Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science "Indian Institute of Science") | Robotics & Autonomous Systems |
| IHUB NTIHAC Foundation | [IIT Kanpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kanpur "IIT Kanpur") | Cyber Security and Cyber Security for Physical Infrastructure |
| IHUB Drishti Foundation | [IIT Jodhpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Jodhpur "IIT Jodhpur") | [Computer vision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_vision "Computer vision"), [Augmented](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Augmented_reality "Augmented reality") and [virtual reality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Virtual_reality "Virtual reality") |
| Divyasampark IHUB Roorkee for Devices Materials and Technology Foundation | [IIT Roorkee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Roorkee "IIT Roorkee") | Device Technology and Materials |
| IIT Patna Vishlesan I-hub Foundation | [IIT Patna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Patna "IIT Patna") | Speech, Video & Text Analytics |
| IIT Madras Pravartak Technologies Foundation | [IIT Madras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras") | Sensors, Networking, Actuator& controls |
| NMICPS Technology Innovation Hub on Autonomous Navigation Foundation (TiHAN) | [IIT Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad") | Autonomous Navigation and Data Acquisition systems |
| I-DAPT-HUB Foundation | [IIT (BHU) Varanasi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_(BHU)_Varanasi "IIT (BHU) Varanasi") | Data Analytics & Predictive Technologies |
| IIT Guwahati Technology Innovation and Development Foundation | [IIT Guwahati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Guwahati "IIT Guwahati") | Technologies for underwater exploration |
| IIT Mandi I-Hub and HCI Foundation | [IIT Mandi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Mandi "IIT Mandi") | [Human–computer interaction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human%E2%80%93computer_interaction "Human–computer interaction") |
| I-Hub Foundation for Cobotics | [IIT Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Delhi "IIT Delhi") | [Collaborative robotics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobot "Cobot") |
| IIT Ropar Technology and Innovation Foundation | [IIT Ropar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Ropar "IIT Ropar") | Technologies for agriculture & water. |
| Technology Innovation in Exploration & Mining Foundation | [IIT (ISM) Dhanbad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_(ISM)_Dhanbad "IIT (ISM) Dhanbad") | Technologies for mining |
| IIT Palakkad Technology I-Hub Foundation | [IIT Palakkad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Palakkad "IIT Palakkad") | Intelligent collaborative systems |
| IIITB Comet Foundation | [International Institute of Information Technology, Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Bengaluru "International Institute of Information Technology, Bengaluru") | Advanced communication system |
| BITS BioCYTiH Foundation | [BITS Pilani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BITS_Pilani "BITS Pilani") | Bio-Cyber-Physical Systems |
| IDEAS- Institute of Data Engineering, Analytics and Science Foundation | [Indian Statistical Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Statistical_Institute "Indian Statistical Institute"), [Kolkata](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata") | Data Science, Big Data Analytics and Data curation |
| IITI Drishti CPS Foundation | [IIT Indore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Indore "IIT Indore") | System Simulation, Modelling & Visualization |
| IHUB Anubhuti-IIITD Foundation | [Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indraprastha_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Delhi "Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi") | [Cognitive Computing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cognitive_computing "Cognitive computing")& Social Sensing |
| I-Hub Quantum Technology Foundation | [Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Pune](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science_Education_and_Research,_Pune "Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Pune") | [Quantum technologies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_technology "Quantum technology") |
| IIT Tirupati Navavishkar I-Hub Foundation | [IIT Tirupati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Tirupati "IIT Tirupati") | Positioning & Precision Technologies |
| IIT Bhilai Innovation and Technology Foundation | [IIT Bhilai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Bhilai "IIT Bhilai") | [Fintech](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fintech "Fintech") |

## Legislation

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Legislation")]

India currently does not have specific laws regulating artificial intelligence (AI). However, the Indian government has introduced several initiatives and guidelines aimed at the responsible development and deployment of AI technologies.[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-morganlewis-87)[[88]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-indiabriefing-88) The Indian government has tasked [NITI Aayog](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NITI_Aayog "NITI Aayog"), its apex public policy think tank, with establishing guidelines and policies for AI. In 2018, NITI Aayog released the **National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence**, also known as **#AIForAll**, which focuses on healthcare, agriculture, education, [smart cities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smart_cities "Smart cities"), and [smart mobility](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smart_mobility "Smart mobility").[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-89)

In 2021, NITI Aayog published the "Principles for Responsible AI," addressing ethical considerations for AI deployment in India. These principles cover system considerations, such as decision-making and accountability, and societal considerations, such as the impact of automation on employment. The second part of this document, "Operationalizing Principles for Responsible AI," released in August 2021, focuses on implementing these ethical principles through regulatory and policy interventions, capacity building, and incentivizing ethical practices.[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-morganlewis-87)[[88]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-indiabriefing-88)

In 2023, the Indian government enacted the [Digital Personal Data Protection Act](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_Personal_Data_Protection_Act,_2023 "Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023"), which addresses some privacy concerns related to AI platforms. [The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology") has also issued advisories requiring platforms to obtain explicit permission before deploying unreliable AI models and to label AI-generated content to prevent misuse.[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-morganlewis-87)[[88]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-indiabriefing-88) India is a member of the [Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence (GPAI)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Global_Partnership_on_Artificial_Intelligence "Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence"), which promotes the responsible use of AI through international collaboration. In 2023, the GPAI Summit was held in New Delhi, where experts discussed responsible AI, data governance, and the future of work.[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-morganlewis-87)

Other Indian agencies, such as the [Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bureau_of_Indian_Standards "Bureau of Indian Standards"), are also working on AI policies. BIS has established a committee to propose draft standards for AI, focusing on safety, reliability, and ethical considerations.[[90]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-dataguidance-90) India has not yet enacted specific AI regulations. However, the government has introduced measures to promote innovation and address ethical concerns and risks associated with AI. These efforts aim to support the growth of India's AI ecosystem and ensure responsible AI deployment.[[91]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-indianexpress-91)

India has launched an AI Data Bank aimed at fostering innovation and enhancing national security. This initiative is designed to harness the power of artificial intelligence by providing a centralized repository of data that can be utilized across various sectors, including governance, business, healthcare, education, and space exploration. By facilitating access to crucial information, the AI Data Bank will support research and development efforts, stimulate technological advancements, and bolster the country’s security framework. This strategic move underscores India's commitment to leveraging AI for national progress and safeguarding its interests in an increasingly digital world.[[92]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-92)

## Bharat GPT initiative

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Bharat GPT initiative")]

The Bharat GPT is a non-profit initiative, started in February 2023.[[93]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:10-93) The goal is to develop India focused [multilingual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multilingualism "Multilingualism"), [multimodal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multimodal_learning "Multimodal learning")[large language models](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model") and [generative pre-trained transformer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Generative_pre-trained_transformer "Generative pre-trained transformer"). Together with the applications and implementation frameworks, the Bharat GPT Consortium intends to publish a series of [foundation models](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_model "Foundation model") in order to meet the needs of developers and businesses.[[94]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:8-94) The **Bharat****GPT Consortium** was established in 2022 under the [public-private partnership](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public%E2%80%93private_partnerships_in_India "Public–private partnerships in India") with [IIT Bombay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Bombay "IIT Bombay"), [Jio Platforms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jio_Platforms "Jio Platforms"), [NASSCOM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASSCOM "NASSCOM"), Seetha Mahalaxmi Healthcare, [Digital India Bhashini Division](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhashini#Background "Bhashini") ([Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology")), [IIT Madras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras"), [IIT Mandi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Mandi "IIT Mandi"), [IIT Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad"), [IIT Kanpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kanpur "IIT Kanpur"), [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad"), and [Indian Institute of Management Indore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Indore "Indian Institute of Management Indore") as members.[[94]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:8-94)[[95]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:9-95)

The Bharat GPT development team is led by Professor Ganesh Ramakrishnan of IIT Bombay as principal investigator, with support from Professor Mohan Raghavan and Professor Maunendra Sankar Desarkar of IIT Hyderabad, Professor Rohit Saluja of IIT Mandi, Professor V Kamakoti of IIT Madras, Professor Arnab Bhattacharya of IIT Kanpur, Professor Kshitij Jadhav of IIT Bombay, Professor Aditya Maheshwari of IIM Indore, and Professor Ravi Kiran of IIIT-Hyderabad.[[93]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:10-93) The text-based foundation model will be released first, followed by speech and video models. In addition to licensing commercial use, the effort has been [open-sourcing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open-source_artificial_intelligence "Open-source artificial intelligence") a large portion of its work.[[96]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-96) Bharat GPT is anticipated to be activated by [speech](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speech_recognition "Speech recognition") and [gesture recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gesture_recognition "Gesture recognition").[[97]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-97)

On 27 December 2023, Akash Ambani announced that Jio Platforms and IIT Bombay were collaborating on Bharat GPT program.[[98]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-98) Jio Platforms will assist Bharat GPT with specialized downstream telecom and retail applications by developing smaller, customized models.[[99]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:11-99) By investigating the potential of AI to foster innovation across a range of goods and services, Jio hopes to achieve its larger goal of establishing a whole ecosystem of growth through Bharat GPT.[[100]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-100)[[101]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-101) Bharat GPT Consortium is assisting in the interpretation of official documents, such as court orders, in close collaboration with the [Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Personnel,_Public_Grievances_and_Pensions "Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions").[[95]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:9-95)[[102]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:12-102)

India's first [ChatGPT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ChatGPT "ChatGPT")-style service, will be launched in March 2024 by Bharat GPT Consortium, with assistance from the [Department of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)"), and [Reliance Industries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reliance_Industries "Reliance Industries").[[103]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-103) The four primary areas in which Bharat GPT sees its model operating are healthcare, financial services, governance, and education, supporting 11 [Indian languages](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_languages_(India) "Indian languages (India)").[[104]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-104)

### Hanooman series

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Hanooman series")]

Seetha Mahalaxmi Healthcare (SML) revealed the Hanooman series [LLM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model") in February 2024 in collaboration with the Bharat GPT Consortium. Among the Hanooman series, the first four AI models—1.5 billion, 7 billion, 13 billion, and 40 billion parameters in size—will be made available as [open-source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_source "Open source") after being trained on 22 Indian languages. The ability to generate text-to-text, [text-to-video](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Text-to-video_model "Text-to-video model"), [speech synthesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speech_synthesis "Speech synthesis") and [speech recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speech_recognition "Speech recognition") will be aided by Hanooman's [multimodal learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multimodal_learning "Multimodal learning") capability. SML is negotiating with healthcare organizations, [BFSI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BFSI "BFSI"), and mobile app developers to create customized models by refining the Hanooman series. The healthcare model VizzhyGPT is the first of these refined iterations, having been trained on extensive [multiomics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiomics "Multiomics"), [clinical research](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clinical_research "Clinical research"), and [electronic health record](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electronic_health_record "Electronic health record") data.[[99]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:11-99)

Hanooman's [alpha version](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alpha_version "Alpha version"), which lacks multimodal features and internet access, was launched on 10 May 2024, in 98 international languages, including 12 Indian languages ([Hindi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindi "Hindi"), [Marathi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marathi_language "Marathi language"), [Gujarati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gujarati_language "Gujarati language"), [Bengali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengali_language "Bengali language"), [Kannada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kannada "Kannada"), [Odia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odia_language "Odia language"), [Punjabi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punjabi_language "Punjabi language"), [Assamese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assamese_language "Assamese language"), [Tamil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamil_language "Tamil language"), [Telugu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telugu_language "Telugu language"), [Malayalam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam"), and [Sindhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sindhi_language "Sindhi language")). It enables tutoring, coding, and conversation. The platform is collaborating with [NASSCOM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASSCOM "NASSCOM"), [Hewlett-Packard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hewlett-Packard "Hewlett-Packard"), and the [Government of Telangana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Telangana "Government of Telangana"). [Yotta Infrastructure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yotta_Infrastructure "Yotta Infrastructure") will supply GPU cloud infrastructure. As of 14 May 2024, the Hanooman chatbot is accessible via both a [web-client](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_client "Web client") and an [Android application](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Android_application "Android application").[[102]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:12-102)[[105]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-105)

Hanooman is jointly owned by SML and 3AI Holding Limited, an AI [investment company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Investment_company "Investment company") based in [Abu Dhabi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abu_Dhabi "Abu Dhabi"). Both businesses will own 50% share under joint ownership arrangement. In order to improve Hanooman's text, voice, image, and coding capabilities for users, it will be able to access 3AI Holding's Omega [GenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GenAI "GenAI"), which is being created with 665 billion [parameters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parameter "Parameter") and 20 trillion tokens. For business clients, Hanooman will launch a [proprietary model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proprietary_software "Proprietary software").[[106]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-106)[[107]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-107)

## BharatGen

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: BharatGen")]

BharatGen, formerly BharatGPT, marks the next phase of India’s sovereign AI development efforts. IIT Bombay Professor Ganesh Ramakrishnan thought of creating a homegrown solution that would use [GenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GenAI "GenAI") and take into account the linguistic and cultural diversity of India. An [open-source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_source "Open source"), [multimodal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multimodal_learning "Multimodal learning"), [multilingual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multilingualism "Multilingualism"), India-centric [foundation model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_model "Foundation model") called BharatGen was formally introduced on 30 September 2024. With an investment of less than [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")235 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$28 million), the [Department of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)") is funding the project under National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems. Due to security concerns in mission-critical applications like defense, the project gained strategic importance and aims to reduce reliance on foreign AI models. Professor Ganesh Ramakrishnan has been advocating for a [public–private partnership](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public%E2%80%93private_partnerships_in_India "Public–private partnerships in India") in AI through the Bharat GPT program. The earlier Bharat GPT efforts have taken on a more formal role in the BharatGen project, which is anticipated for completion by 2026.[[108]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:7-108)[[109]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-109)

As a national mission, BharatGen has been set up to facilitate inter-ministerial cooperation by taking [whole-of-government approach](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Whole-of-government_approach "Whole-of-government approach"). The AI will be developed from the ground up, guaranteeing that all [intellectual property](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intellectual_property "Intellectual property") stays in the country. IndiaAI Compute Facility will spearhead the project. BharatGen will have robust academic base, incorporating faculty and research institutions into its ecosystem to guarantee sustained innovation. [Yotta Infrastructure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yotta_Infrastructure "Yotta Infrastructure"), and Neysa are providing [cloud support](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cloud_computing "Cloud computing").[[110]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-110)[[111]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-111)

The [backend](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Backend_(computing) "Backend (computing)")[algorithm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algorithm "Algorithm") development and the necessary technical work was done by a collaborative team from BharatGen consortium. As of February 2025, the BharatGen consortium has 50–60 researchers and a wide range of student contributors from the [Indian Institute of Management Indore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Indore "Indian Institute of Management Indore"), the [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad"), [IIT Bombay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Bombay "IIT Bombay"), [IIT Kanpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kanpur "IIT Kanpur"), [IIT Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad"), [IIT Mandi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Mandi "IIT Mandi"), and [IIT Madras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras"). BharatGen consortium will work with behavioral economists, engineers, AI researchers, and design specialists, to develop a multidisciplinary strategy to use AI to address India's problems. The AI model takes into account the 1,600 languages and scripts that make up the Indian knowledge system. Each institute of the consortium is investigating particular tasks to create models in [voice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speech_recognition "Speech recognition"), [language](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Language_model "Language model"), and [vision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_vision "Computer vision").[[112]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-112)[[113]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:6-113)

As of 30 January 2025, the framework for the AI model is ready. The development team has spent the last one and a half years working on the project. The initial version should be available in four to ten months, according to [Ashwini Vaishnaw](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashwini_Vaishnaw "Ashwini Vaishnaw"), Minister of Electronics and IT.[[114]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-114)

In order to concentrate on primary data collection, BharatGen started the [Bharat Data Sagar](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bharat_Data_Sagar&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bharat Data Sagar (page does not exist)") initiative, a multilingual repository for AI research. The goal of this data collection is to satisfy the need for training data for Indian languages that are underrepresented in [data corpora](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_corpus "Data corpus"). It will capture the Indian linguistic nuances, which are frequently disregarded in international AI models. BharatGen offers tools and technologies to facilitate the creation of region specific tailored content by translating between local languages and dialects. In order to accurately portray India's languages, dialects, and cultural context, BharatGen focuses on gathering and curating data unique to the nation.[[108]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:7-108)[[113]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:6-113)

Using [vision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_vision "Computer vision") language models, BharatGen launched e-vikrAI in October 2024. It makes e-commerce easier for non-English speaking vendors by automating the cataloguing process and doing away with the need for human input. Sellers only need to provide an image of their product to get auto-generated titles, descriptions, features, and pricing recommendations. e-vikrAI improves accessibility by translating and vocalizing product descriptions in Indian languages.[[115]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-115)[[116]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-116)

## IndiaAI Mission and compute facility

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: IndiaAI Mission and compute facility")]

The **IndiaAI Mission** was approved in 7 March 2024 consisting of seven core pillars, and an allocation of [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")10,371.92 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$1.2 billion) across various components as follows:

| Core pillars | Fund allocation for 2024-2029 |
| --- | --- |
| IndiaAI Compute | [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")4,563.36 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$540 million) |
| IndiaAI Datasets Platform | [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")199.55 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$24 million) |
| IndiaAI Application Development Initiatives | [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")689.05 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$82 million) |
| IndiaAI FutureSkills | [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")882.94 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$100 million) |
| IndiaAI Innovation Center | [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")1,971.37 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$230 million) |
| IndiaAI Startup Financing | [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")1,942.5 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$230 million) |
| Safe & Trusted AI | [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")20.46 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$2.4 million) |

Additionally, IndiaAI Overheads and Contingency received funding of [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_rupee "Indian rupee")102.69 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") (US$12 million).[[117]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-117) The [digital public infrastructure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_public_infrastructure "Digital public infrastructure") framework for AI in India guarantees consent-based, ethically generated datasets while lowering dependency on foreign and [synthetic data](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Synthetic_data "Synthetic data").[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:14-118)

On 30 January 2025, [Ashwini Vaishnaw](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashwini_Vaishnaw "Ashwini Vaishnaw"), the [Minister for Electronics and information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology"), confirmed that the IndiaAI Mission would customize native AI solutions for the Indian context using [Indian languages](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_languages_(India) "Indian languages (India)"), supported by a state-of-the-art shared computing infrastructure. The initial AI model starts with a compute capacity of about 10,000 [GPUs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPUs "GPUs"), with the remaining 8693 GPUs to be added shortly. The facility includes 7,200 [AMD Instinct MI200](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AMD_Instinct#Products "AMD Instinct") and [MI300 series](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AMD_Instinct#Products "AMD Instinct"), 12,896 [Nvidia H100](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hopper_(microarchitecture)#H100_accelerator_and_DGX_H100 "Hopper (microarchitecture)"), and 1,480 [H200](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hopper_(microarchitecture)#H100_accelerator_and_DGX_H100 "Hopper (microarchitecture)") processors. The cost of computing the AI model will be less than [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E2%82%B9 "₹")100 per hour following a 40% government subsidy, with half-yearly and annual plans for developers, researchers, and students.[[119]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:5-119)[[120]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-120)

Startups, MSMEs, universities, researchers, students, and governmental organizations will all be able to access AI compute, network, storage, platform, and cloud services through the IndiaAI Compute Portal. Easy access to Nvidia H100, H200, [A100](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A100_GPU "A100 GPU"), [L40S](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Nvidia_graphics_processing_units#Tesla "List of Nvidia graphics processing units"), and [L4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Nvidia_graphics_processing_units#Tesla "List of Nvidia graphics processing units"), [AMD MI300x](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AMD_Instinct#MI300_series "AMD Instinct") and [325X](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AMD_Instinct#Products "AMD Instinct"), [Intel Gaudi 2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intel#'IDM_2.0'_strategy "Intel"), [AWS Trainium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AWS_Trainium "AWS Trainium"), and [Inferentia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inferentia "Inferentia") will be made possible by the portal. Nearly 45% of the IndiaAI Mission's budget goes into the AI Compute Portal.[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:14-118)

Accessing more expensive GPUs would cost ₹150 per hour, while utilizing less expensive GPUs would cost ₹115.85 per hour. Orient Technologies, CMS Computers Limited, SHI Locuz, CtrlS, NxtGen Cloud Technologies, [Yotta Infrastructure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yotta_Infrastructure "Yotta Infrastructure"), [Jio Platforms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jio_Platforms "Jio Platforms"), [Tata Communications](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tata_Communications "Tata Communications"), E2E Networks, and Vensysco Technologies have been approved by the government to provide 18,693 GPUs.[[121]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-121)

As part of the IndianAI Mission, the [union government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Union_government "Union government"), in April 2025, selected [Sarvam AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarvam_AI "Sarvam AI"), to develop the country's first sovereign [LLM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LLM "LLM"), which will include the capabilities of reasoning, voice, and fluency in Indian languages.[[122]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-122)[[123]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-123) As of May 2025, the government, as part of the IndianAI Mission, is supporting Indian researchers in designing indigenous GPUs.[[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-124)

Ashwini Vaishnaw announced AIKosha: IndiaAI Datasets Platform, the AI Compute Portal, the AI Competency Framework for Public Sector Officials, iGOT-AI Mission Karmayogi, the IndiaAI Startups Global Acceleration Program with [Station F](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Station_F "Station F"), the IndiaAI Application Development Initiative, and IndiaAI FutureSkills on 6 March 2025, as part of the IndiaAI Mission to strengthen AI-driven research, innovation, and skill development.
