For public officials, the iGOT-AI Mission Karmayogi incorporates AI-driven learning recommendations.

**AIKosha: IndiaAI Datasets Platform**

To facilitate AI innovation, AIKosha will offer a safe, centralized platform with easy access to a database of models, use cases, and datasets. Through an integrated development environment, tools, and tutorials, it offers AI [sandbox](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandbox_(software_development) "Sandbox (software development)") capabilities. AIKosha has features like permission-based access, content discoverability, AI readiness scoring of datasets, and security methods including secure [APIs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/API "API"), [firewalls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firewall_(computing) "Firewall (computing)") for real-time harmful traffic filtering, and [data encryption](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_encryption "Data encryption") both in motion and at rest. More than 80 models and 300 datasets are available on AIKosha.[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:14-118) Both the public and private sector organizations gather AIKosha datasets, which include [census data](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Census_of_India "Census of India"), [geospatial data](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geospatial_data "Geospatial data"), and [linguistic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linguistics "Linguistics") data.[[125]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:15-125)

**IndiaAI Startups Global Acceleration Program**

The IndiaAI Mission will begin a four-month acceleration program in partnership with [Station F](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Station_F "Station F") and [HEC Paris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HEC_Paris "HEC Paris") from 2025. The ten shortlisted AI [startup companies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Startup_companies "Startup companies") will have access to networking opportunities, mentorship, and international market expansion in [Europe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Europe "Europe").[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:14-118)

### AI Research Analytics and Knowledge Dissemination Platform

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: AI Research Analytics and Knowledge Dissemination Platform")]

With a peak performance of 13,170 [teraflops](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teraflops "Teraflops"), AIRAWAT-PSAI is the biggest and fastest AI supercomputing machine in India. It was deployed at [C-DAC, Pune](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C-DAC "C-DAC") in 2023 and is maintained by Netweb Technologies.[[126]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-126)

[MeitY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeitY "MeitY") is also funding the implementation of 200 AI [Petaflops](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Petaflops "Petaflops")[Mixed Precision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mixed-precision_arithmetic "Mixed-precision arithmetic") peak computing capacity (scalable to 790 AI Petaflops) [Proof of Concept](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proof_of_concept "Proof of concept") for AI Research Analytics and Knowledge Dissemination Platform (AIRAWAT), which is being carried out by C-DAC, Pune. A combined peak compute of 410 AI Petaflops Mixed Precision and a sustained compute capability of 8.5 Petaflops ([Rmax](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rmax_(computing) "Rmax (computing)")) [Double Precision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Double-precision_floating-point_format "Double-precision floating-point format") are achieved by combining the 200 AI Petaflop AIRAWAT PoC with the 210 AI Petaflop [PARAM Siddhi-AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PARAM_Siddhi-AI "PARAM Siddhi-AI"). 13 Petaflops is the maximum computation capacity (Double Precision, Rpeak). A plan for expanding AIRAWAT to 1,000 AI Petaflops of Mixed Precision computing capacity has been envisioned by MeitY. With its extensive, power-optimized AI cloud infrastructure, AIRAWAT will serve as a common computational cloud platform for Big Data Analytics and Assimilation, linking all Centers for Research Excellence in Artificial Intelligence (COREs), Indian Centres for Transformational AI (ICTAIs), and other academic, research lab, scientific community, industry, and start-up institutions with the National Knowledge Network.

With an emphasis on [NLP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NLP_(computer_science) "NLP (computer science)"), surveillance and [image processing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Image_processing "Image processing"), [pattern recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pattern_recognition "Pattern recognition"), [medical imaging](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Medical_imaging "Medical imaging"), education, agriculture, finance, healthcare, audio assistance, robotics, national security, defense, the automotive industry, supply chain management, [human resource development](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_resource_development "Human resource development"), and anomalous behavior detection from [video analytics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Video_analytics "Video analytics"), AIRAWAT will support the field of applied AI. Deployed under the [National Supercomputing Mission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Supercomputing_Mission "National Supercomputing Mission"), it will support the [Digital India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_India "Digital India")[Bhashini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhashini "Bhashini") initiative and accomplish the goals of the National Program on AI.[[127]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-127)[[128]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-128)[[129]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-129)

## International collaboration

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: International collaboration")]

[Prime Minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_India "Prime Minister of India")[Narendra Modi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Narendra_Modi "Narendra Modi") with [NITI Aayog](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NITI_Aayog "NITI Aayog") officially opened the [World Economic Forum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Economic_Forum "World Economic Forum")'s Center for the Fourth Industrial Revolution liaison office in [Navi Mumbai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Navi_Mumbai "Navi Mumbai") on 11 October 2018.[[130]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-130)[[131]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-131) It's objective is to foster the responsible adoption and deployment of new technologies by designing policy frameworks and protocols with the government, corporations, academic institutions, [startups](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Startups "Startups"), and international organizations. The Indian team will collaborate with their counterparts in [Beijing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), [Tokyo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), and [San Francisco](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco") to focus on [blockchain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blockchain "Blockchain")&[DLT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distributed_ledger "Distributed ledger"), AI &[ML](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning "Machine learning").[[132]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-132) To increase farmers' incomes, [C4IR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Economic_Forum#Organization "World Economic Forum") has been working on AI-driven agricultural initiatives since 2018. In January 2024, C4IR, [MeitY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeitY "MeitY"), [NASSCOM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASSCOM "NASSCOM"), and the [Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Principal_Scientific_Adviser_to_the_Government_of_India "Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India") launched the **AI for India 2030 initiative**. Its goal is to offer a systematic approach to the ethical, inclusive, and responsible adoption of AI in India.[[133]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-133)[[134]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-134)[[135]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-135)

India's [Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology") and Japan's [Ministry of Economy, Trade, and Industry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Economy,_Trade_and_Industry "Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry") signed a memorandum of cooperation on 29 October 2018, which outlines the two nations' cooperation in AI and [IoT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IoT "IoT") as part of **India-Japan Cooperation on Digital Partnership**. In an effort to strengthen ties between NITI Aayog and [METI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Economy,_Trade_and_Industry "Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry"), India and Japan signed a broad statement of intent on AI under "Society 5.0" that included the exploration of potential institutional collaborations, such as joint projects between [IIT Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad") and Artificial Intelligence Research Centre ([National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Institute_of_Advanced_Industrial_Science_and_Technology "National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology")), related to [machine learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning "Machine learning"), [deep learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_learning "Deep learning"), [data mining](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_mining "Data mining"), and other AI themes. Joint scientific and technological cooperation in [ML](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning "Machine learning"), and [probabilistic logic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Probabilistic_logic "Probabilistic logic") techniques for various data types and combinations were added to the extended MoU on 24 December 2021.[[136]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-136)[[137]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-137)

In 2021, the Indian [Department of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)") and the [United States Department of State](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Department_of_State "United States Department of State") were designated as the nodal agencies for the **US India Artificial Intelligence Initiative** launched under Indo-US Science and Technology Forum.[[138]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-138)[[139]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-139) It will provide a forum for bilateral research and development cooperation. It will also facilitate AI innovation, exchange ideas for building an AI workforce, and suggest ways to promote collaborations.[[140]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-140)[[141]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-141)[[142]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-142)

The [United States](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States "United States") and India expanded their joint cyber training and exercises in 2022 and initiated the **Defense Artificial Intelligence Dialogue**.[[143]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-143)[[144]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-144) According to [Cleo Paskal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cleo_Paskal "Cleo Paskal"), a non-resident senior fellow at the [Foundation for Defense of Democracies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_for_Defense_of_Democracies "Foundation for Defense of Democracies"), the Indian community has influenced AI research and development in the US for many years. Things are taken to the next level by the Defense Artificial Intelligence Dialogue.[[145]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-145) In the same year, the US intends to join six of India's Technology Innovation Hubs to support collaborative research projects in fields including AI, advanced wireless technologies, and data science to further advancements in applications like agriculture, health, and climate. Thirty-five projects have been selected for implementation by the [DST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Department of Science and Technology (India)") and [U.S. National Science Foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._National_Science_Foundation "U.S. National Science Foundation").[[146]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-146)[[147]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-147)

As part of the [United States–India Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States%E2%80%93India_Initiative_on_Critical_and_Emerging_Technology "United States–India Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technology"), the US and India announced setting aside more than $2 million in 2024 for collaborative research initiatives that will advance AI and [quantum technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_technology "Quantum technology").[[148]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-148)[[149]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-149)

In order to develop [advanced driver-assistance systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Advanced_driver-assistance_system "Advanced driver-assistance system") and [vehicular automation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vehicular_automation "Vehicular automation") technologies that can be used in different parts of the world, the Japanese business [Honda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Honda "Honda") began collaborative research on AI technologies with [IIT Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Delhi "IIT Delhi") and [IIT Bombay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Bombay "IIT Bombay") in 2024. This was done in order to further advance the company's own Honda Cooperative Intelligence AI platform.[[150]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-150)[[151]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-151)

To create safe, open, secure, and reliable AI, Prime Minister Narendra Modi and [President](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_France "President of France")[Emmanuel Macron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emmanuel_Macron "Emmanuel Macron") announced the **India-France Roadmap on Artificial Intelligence** on 12 February 2025.[[152]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-152)[[153]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-153) The goal is to make sure that the rules and guidelines governing the use of AI represent democratic principles and maximize its potential for the advancement of humanity and the common good.[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-154)[[155]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-155)

The [India-EU Trade and Technology Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=India-EU_Trade_and_Technology_Council&action=edit&redlink=1 "India-EU Trade and Technology Council (page does not exist)") has made AI a top priority. In 2025, the [European AI Office](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=European_AI_Office&action=edit&redlink=1 "European AI Office (page does not exist)") and the India AI Mission agreed to strengthen collaboration on [large language models](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model"), including collaborative efforts to create frameworks and tools for responsible and ethical AI. It will expand upon the advancements in [high-performance computing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-performance_computing "High-performance computing") applications related to [bioinformatics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bioinformatics "Bioinformatics"), [climate change](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Climate_change "Climate change"), and [natural disasters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Natural_disaster "Natural disaster") that have been made possible by collaborative [research and development](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Research_and_development "Research and development").[[156]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-156)[[157]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-157)

On 2 July 2025, the [Government of Telangana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Telangana "Government of Telangana") launched the Telangana Data Exchange (TGDeX) in partnership with [IISc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IISc "IISc") and the [Japan International Cooperation Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Japan_International_Cooperation_Agency "Japan International Cooperation Agency"). It will enable access to structured datasets and developer tools required to create AI solutions. TGDeX will utilize Open Data Telangana platform. TGDeX's goal of hosting more than 2,000 AI-ready datasets over the next five years will be aided by the Telangana AI Mission (T-AIM), [T–Hub](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/T%E2%80%93Hub "T–Hub"), MATH (AI/ML Centre of Excellence of T-Hub), IIT Hyderabad, and [IIIT-Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIIT_Hyderabad "IIIT Hyderabad").[[158]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-158)[[159]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-159)[[160]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-160) Launched in August 2025, the **India-Japan AI Cooperation Initiative** aims to advance strategic cooperation in AI through cooperative research, university-company partnerships, [LLM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LLM "LLM") development, and cooperation in building a reliable AI ecosystem.[[161]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-161)[[162]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-162)

## Impact

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Impact")]

### Agriculture

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Agriculture")]

On 12 December 2023, KissanAI announced the release of Dhenu 1.0, world's first agriculture-specific [LLM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model") designed for Indian farmers.[[163]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-163) It can handle 300,000 instruction sets and understand English, [Hindi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindi "Hindi"), and [Hinglish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hinglish "Hinglish") queries. The platform provides voice-based, tailored assistance. KissanAI used Sarvam AI's OpenHathi for [bilingual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bilingual "Bilingual") support and worked with NimbleBox for [APIs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/API "API"). Dhenu 1.0 utilizes OpenHermes 2.5 [Mistral 7B](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mistral_AI#Models "Mistral AI"). KissanAI is supported by [Microsoft for Startups](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microsoft_for_Startups "Microsoft for Startups").[[164]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-164)[[165]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-165) For climate resilient agriculture practices, KissanAI partnered with the [United Nations Development Programme](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Nations_Development_Programme "United Nations Development Programme") in January 2024 to develop a voice-based [vernacular](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vernacular "Vernacular")[GenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GenAI "GenAI") virtual assistant. The main goals of this project are to overcome language barriers and offer easily comprehensible information so that female farmers can actively engage in and profit from generative AI developments.[[166]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-166)[[167]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-167) Dhenu2, an [open-source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_source "Open source") version, was launched in October 2024, providing solutions for agribusinesses, farmers, and policymakers worldwide. It is available in three models: 8B, 3B, and 1B, and is powered by the [Llama 3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Llama_3 "Llama 3"). The platform is based on 1.5 million synthetic and real-world instructions that cover more than 4,000 agricultural topics and categories. Over 100,000 farmers were interviewed to gain insights that provided practical information for better decision-making.[[168]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-168)

Uttar Pradesh Open Network for Agriculture, a [digital public infrastructure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_public_infrastructure "Digital public infrastructure"), powered by [Gemini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gemini_(language_model) "Gemini (language model)") and [Beckn protocol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_Network_for_Digital_Commerce#Beckn_Protocol "Open Network for Digital Commerce"), was launched in January 2025 by the [Government of Uttar Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Uttar_Pradesh "Government of Uttar Pradesh") in collaboration with [Google Cloud Platform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Google_Cloud_Platform "Google Cloud Platform"). Farmers will get one-stop access to market linkages, loans, mechanization, and advisory services for selling their produce. [Voice user interface](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voice_user_interface "Voice user interface") will be available to farmers in Hindi, [Bengali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengali_language "Bengali language"), [Telugu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telugu_language "Telugu language"), [Kannada](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kannada "Kannada"), [Gujarati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gujarati_language "Gujarati language"), and [Punjabi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punjabi_language "Punjabi language"), with other [Indian languages](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_languages_(India) "Indian languages (India)") to be added in 2025.[[169]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-169)

AgriHub, a center of excellence for [sustainable agriculture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sustainable_agriculture "Sustainable agriculture") co-funded by the [MeitY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeitY "MeitY") and the [Government of Madhya Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Madhya_Pradesh "Government of Madhya Pradesh"), was opened by [IIT Indore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Indore "IIT Indore") on 27 January 2025. It will use AI, machine learning, and [deep learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_learning "Deep learning") to revolutionize the agricultural industry. By using big data analytics and [genomic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Genomics "Genomics") research to support data-driven agriculture, it will enable research in [precision agriculture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Precision_agriculture "Precision agriculture"), [xerophyte](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xerophyte "Xerophyte"), and AI-driven disease diagnostics.[[170]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-170)

Farmers in [Fazilka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fazilka_district "Fazilka district") have embraced AI-powered weather sensors created by [IIT Ropar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Ropar "IIT Ropar"). It provides real-time information on atmospheric conditions by utilizing data gathered from satellites, weather balloons and a network of weather stations on the ground. Parameters like temperature, humidity, precipitation, and lux are used with data from the [India Meteorological Department](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India_Meteorological_Department "India Meteorological Department") to forecast rainfall.[[171]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-171)

AI is being used by the [Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Agriculture_and_Farmers%27_Welfare "Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare") to assist farmers. An AI-powered chatbot called Kisan e-Mitra was built to help farmers with their questions on [Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pradhan_Mantri_Kisan_Samman_Nidhi "Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi") scheme in multiple Indian languages. The [National Pest Surveillance System](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=National_Pest_Surveillance_System&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Pest Surveillance System (page does not exist)") uses AI and [ML](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning "Machine learning") to identify pest infestation, allowing for prompt action for healthier crops and addressing the loss of produce brought on by climate change. AI-based analytics that use field photos to monitor and evaluate crop health utilizing weather, soil moisture, and satellite data for [wheat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wheat "Wheat") and [rice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice "Rice") cultivation.[[172]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-172)

The use of AI in Khutbav village by the Agriculture Development Trust and [Microsoft](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft") in 2025 led to a 40% increase in yield and a 50% reduction in production costs due to water and pesticide usage. Through a smartphone application Agripilot.ai developed by Click2Cloud, the AI notify farmers of possible pest attacks, soil conditions, air speed, and weather conditions to determine when to apply pesticides and how much water the crop requires. Utilizing a variety of data sources, including [satellite imagery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Satellite_imagery "Satellite imagery"), [weather forecasts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Weather_forecasts "Weather forecasts"), soil sensors, and inputs unique to each farm, the AI generate personalized suggestions for farmers. Microsoft's Azure Data Manager for Agriculture (formerly Microsoft FarmBeats), is used to process this data. The annual fee for AI services is ₹10,000.[[173]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-173) In order to offer insights, the data is analyzed by the open-source research project FarmVibes.ai in conjunction with historical crop data. For the farmer, [Microsoft Azure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microsoft_Azure "Microsoft Azure")[OpenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAI "OpenAI") Service transforms these details into easy-to-follow steps.[[174]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-174)

In 100 villages, the [Assam government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assam_Government "Assam Government") will support the establishment of AI-powered agri-hubs, where drones and AI would assist farmers in making data-driven decisions, increasing yields, and promoting sustainability, according to the state budget for 2025–2026.[[175]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-175)

### Industry

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Industry")]

With an investment of ₹20 crore, [Tata Consultancy Services](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tata_Consultancy_Services "Tata Consultancy Services") founded the FC Kohli Centre on Intelligent Systems at [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad") in 2015 to conduct research in the fields of [robotics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robotics "Robotics"), cognitive sciences, neural language processing, and intelligent systems.[[176]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-176)[[177]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-177)[[178]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-178)

In March 2018, [Capillary Technologies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Capillary_Technologies "Capillary Technologies") and [IIT Kharagpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kharagpur "IIT Kharagpur") established the Centre of Excellence in Artificial Intelligence Research for financial analytics, [industrial automation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industrial_automation "Industrial automation"), digital healthcare, and [intelligent transportation systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intelligent_transportation_system "Intelligent transportation system"), with a combined investment of [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E2%82%B9 "₹")56.4 million.[[179]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-179)[[180]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-180) By March 2019, a second campus at Kukatpallya will open.[[181]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-181) In July, the [Karnataka government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karnataka_government "Karnataka government") and [NASSCOM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASSCOM "NASSCOM") jointly established the Center of Excellence for Data Science and Artificial Intelligence on a [public–private partnership](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public%E2%80%93private_partnerships_in_India "Public–private partnerships in India") for helping [small and medium enterprises](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Small_and_medium_enterprises "Small and medium enterprises") and make big data sets for training models available.[[182]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-182)[[183]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-183)

For fundamental research in [deep learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_learning "Deep learning"), [reinforcement learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reinforcement_learning "Reinforcement learning"), network analytics, interpretable machine learning, and domain-aware AI, [Bosch](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bosch_(company) "Bosch (company)") established the [Robert Bosch](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Bosch "Robert Bosch") Center for Data Science and Artificial Intelligence at IIT Madras in 2019.[[184]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-184)[[185]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-185) The center will concentrate its applied research on [systems biology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Systems_biology "Systems biology"), [smart cities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smart_cities "Smart cities"), manufacturing analytics, financial analytics, and healthcare. Additionally, it is the location of India's largest [deep reinforcement learning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_reinforcement_learning "Deep reinforcement learning") group.[[186]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-186) At the Microsoft Research Lab in [Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengaluru "Bengaluru"), Microsoft introduced Societal Impact through Cloud and Artificial Intelligence in 2019 to use cloud computing and AI to address long-term societal issues in transportation, agriculture, health and wellness, and education.[[187]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-187)

[Accenture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Accenture "Accenture") opened its first Nano Lab in the [Asia–Pacific](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asia%E2%80%93Pacific "Asia–Pacific") on 4 February 2020, in [Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabad "Hyderabad"), for applied research in security, [extended reality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extended_reality "Extended reality"), and AI.[[188]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-188) The [Nvidia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nvidia "Nvidia") AI Technology Center was founded at [IIT Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Hyderabad "IIT Hyderabad") in 2020 with the goal of accelerating AI research and commercialization.[[189]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-189) It focuses on the application of AI in language comprehension, smart cities, and agriculture.[[190]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-190)[Situational awareness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Situation_awareness "Situation awareness"), operator environment analysis, [smart appliances](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Smart_appliance "Smart appliance"), [autonomous navigation systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autonomous_Navigation_System "Autonomous Navigation System"), [edge devices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edge_device "Edge device") for [IoT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IoT "IoT") and [industry 4.0](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industry_4.0 "Industry 4.0"), and [conversational user interfaces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conversational_user_interface "Conversational user interface") were among the AI applications that [Tata Elxsi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tata_Elxsi "Tata Elxsi")'s Artificial Intelligence Centre of Excellence began deploying in 2020.[[191]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-191) In 2020, [Intel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intel "Intel") established INAI, an applied artificial intelligence research center in [Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabad "Hyderabad"), in partnership with the [Public Health Foundation of India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_Health_Foundation_of_India "Public Health Foundation of India"), [IIIT-H](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIIT-H "IIIT-H"), and the [Government of Telangana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Telangana "Government of Telangana"). INAI will focus on population-level issues in the smart mobility and healthcare sectors.[[192]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-192)

SiMa.ai established a new design center in Bengaluru in 2021 to work on the MLSoC platform, the first machine learning [SoC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/System_on_a_chip "System on a chip") specifically designed for the industry. This platform can support any framework, [neural network](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neural_network_(machine_learning) "Neural network (machine learning)"), or [foundational model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_model "Foundation model") for any workload.[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-193)

In 2021, [Kotak Mahindra Bank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kotak_Mahindra_Bank "Kotak Mahindra Bank") and IISc announced their intention to establish the Kotak-IISc AI-ML Center for research and innovation in [fintech](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fintech "Fintech"), as well as to cultivate the necessary talent pool to satisfy industry demands.[[194]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-194)[[195]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-195) Private businesses are attempting to create smaller, less expensive [large language model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model"). Examples of these include AI4Bharat's Airawat series, Sarvam AI's OpenHathi series, CoRover.ai's BharatGPT, [Tech Mahindra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tech_Mahindra "Tech Mahindra")'s Indus project, [Ola](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ola_Consumer "Ola Consumer")'s Krutrim, TWO AI's Sutra series, and SML's series.[[196]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-196)[[197]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-197)

[IIT Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Delhi "IIT Delhi")'s Yardi School of Artificial Intelligence and an Indian [deep tech](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_tech "Deep tech") firm KnowDis are working together to create AI models that could find possible [antibodies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antibodies "Antibodies") to treat a variety of illnesses.[[198]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-198)[[199]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-199) In part of its $1 billion pledge to accelerate AI-led innovation for the ai360 ecosystem, [Wipro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wipro "Wipro") announced on 18 August 2023, the opening of a Center of Excellence in Generative Artificial Intelligence in collaboration with IIT Delhi.[[200]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-200)[[201]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-201) To study AI, the [Adani Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adani_Group "Adani Group") and the [International Holding Company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Holding_Company "International Holding Company") established a joint venture on 28 December 2023.[[202]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-202)

On 29 August 2024, [Mukesh Ambani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mukesh_Ambani "Mukesh Ambani") revealed the creation of an application agnostic suite of tools and platforms called Jio Brain, to hasten the [Reliance Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reliance_Group "Reliance Group")'s adoption of AI and set the stage for [6G](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/6G "6G") development with plans to offer services to [vendors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vendor "Vendor"). JioBrain combines high speed and low latency of [5G](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/5G "5G") with automation, [anomaly detection](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anomaly_detection "Anomaly detection"), and predictive forecasting. Reliance intends to use it to optimize processes in education, healthcare, entertainment, retail, and agriculture.[[203]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-203)[[204]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-204)[[205]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-205) JioBrain includes over 500 [REST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/REST "REST")[APIs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/API "API") and data APIs.[[206]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-206) It can be used for [NLP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NLP_(computer_science) "NLP (computer science)"), image-to-video, text-to-music, text-to-image and video, speech-to-speech, speech-to-text translation, code generation, explanation, optimization, and debugging. Additionally, it contains [feature engineering](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Feature_engineering "Feature engineering"), model chaining, and [hyperparameter optimization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyperparameter_optimization "Hyperparameter optimization").[[207]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-207) Jio Brain offers mobile and enterprise-ready [LLM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LLM "LLM")-as-a-service capability for [GenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GenAI "GenAI").[[208]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-208)

AI has been used in medical devices, medical reports, predicting advertising & marketing results, do product design & development, healthcare & cognitive testing with diagnostic AI. GenAI is being used by Indian brands for product ideation, visual concept development, social post creation.[[209]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-209)[[210]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-210)

In 2025, [Citadel Securities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Citadel_Securities "Citadel Securities") and [IIT Kanpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Kanpur "IIT Kanpur") announced a collaboration to construct the Translational and Transformative Training and Investigations Laboratory, an advanced [GPU](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPU "GPU") research facility for work on machine learning, intelligent systems, data science, [data visualization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_visualization "Data visualization"), translational AI, and [high-performance computing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-performance_computing "High-performance computing").[[211]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-211)[Project Waterworth](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Project_Waterworth&action=edit&redlink=1 "Project Waterworth (page does not exist)"), a 50,000 km undersea cable for high-speed connectivity required to propel AI innovation, was unveiled by Meta on 14 February 2025. It will link South Africa, Brazil, India, the United States, and other regions.[[212]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-212) India is among the nations with the highest usage of [Meta AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meta_AI "Meta AI") on [WhatsApp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WhatsApp "WhatsApp") and [Instagram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Instagram "Instagram").[[213]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-213)

### Defence

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Defence")]

A task force for the Strategic Implementation of AI for National Security and Defence was established in February 2018 by the [Ministry of Defense](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(India) "Ministry of Defence (India)")'s Department of Defence Production.[[214]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-214) The process of getting the military ready for AI use was started by the [MoD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(India) "Ministry of Defence (India)") in 2019.[[215]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-215) The [Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Robotics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centre_for_Artificial_Intelligence_and_Robotics "Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Robotics") was approved to develop AI solutions for [signal intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Signals_intelligence "Signals intelligence") to improve intelligence collection and analysis capabilities at a cost of [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E2%82%B9 "₹")73.9 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") and Energy Harvesting Based Infrared Sensor Network for Automated Human Intrusion Detection (EYESIRa) at a cost of ₹1.8 crore.[[216]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-216) In 2021, the [Indian Army](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Army "Indian Army"), with assistance from the [National Security Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Security_Council_(India) "National Security Council (India)"), began operating the Quantum Lab and Artificial Intelligence Center at the [Military College of Telecommunication Engineering](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Military_College_of_Telecommunication_Engineering "Military College of Telecommunication Engineering"). With an emphasis on robotics and artificial intelligence, [Defence Research and Development Organisation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Defence_Research_and_Development_Organisation "Defence Research and Development Organisation") and [Indian Institute of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science "Indian Institute of Science") established the Joint Advanced Technology Programme-Center of Excellence.[[217]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-217)[[218]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-218) In 2022, the [Indian Navy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Navy "Indian Navy") created an AI Core group and set up a Center of Excellence for AI and [Big Data analysis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Big_data_analysis "Big data analysis") at [INS Valsura](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INS_Valsura "INS Valsura").[[219]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-219)[[220]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-220) Indian Army incubated Artificial Intelligence Offensive Drone Operations Project in partnership with an Indian startup.[[221]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-221)[[222]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-222) Tonbo Imaging integrated real-time target identification and [Edge AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edge_AI "Edge AI")[image processing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Image_processing "Image processing") into [MPATGM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MPATGM "MPATGM").[[223]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-223) During Exercise Dakshin Shakti 2021, the Indian Army integrated AI into its [intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Military_intelligence "Military intelligence"), [surveillance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surveillance "Surveillance"), and [reconnaissance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reconnaissance "Reconnaissance") architecture to provide a cohesive operational intelligence picture of the battlefield.[[224]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-224)

In 2022, the Indian government established the **Defence Artificial Intelligence Council** and the **Defence AI Project Agency**,[[225]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-225)[[226]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-226) and it also published a list of 75 defense-related AI priority projects.[[227]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-227)[[228]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-228)[MoD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(India) "Ministry of Defence (India)") earmarked ₹1,000 crore annually till 2026 for capacity building, infrastructure setup, data preparation, and Al project implementation.[[229]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-229) The Indian Army, the Indian Navy and the [Indian Air Force](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Air_Force "Indian Air Force") set aside ₹100 crore annually for the development of AI-specific applications.[[230]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-230) The military is already deploying some AI-enabled projects and equipment.[[231]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-231)[[232]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-232) At Air Force Station Rajokri, the [IAF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Air_Force "Indian Air Force") Centre of Excellence for Artificial Intelligence was established in 2022 as part of the Unit for Digitization, Automation, Artificial Intelligence, and Application Networking (UDAAN).[[233]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-233)[Swarm drone systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Swarm_robotics "Swarm robotics") were introduced by the [Mechanised Infantry Regiment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mechanised_Infantry_Regiment "Mechanised Infantry Regiment") for offensive operations close to [LAC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Line_of_Actual_Control "Line of Actual Control").[[234]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-234)

Grene Robotics revealed Indrajaal Autonomous Drone Defense Dome.[[235]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-235) For offensive operations, the military began acquiring AI-enabled [UAVs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UAVs "UAVs") and [swarm drones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Swarm_drone "Swarm drone").[[236]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-236)[[237]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-237)[[238]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-238)[Bharat Electronics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bharat_Electronics "Bharat Electronics") developed AI-enabled audio transcription and analysis software for battlefield communication. Using AI during transport operations, the Indian Army's Research & Development branch patented driver tiredness monitoring system.[[239]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-239)[[240]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-240) As part of initial investment, the [Indian Armed Forces](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Armed_Forces "Indian Armed Forces") is investing about $50 million (€47.2 million) yearly on AI, according to Delhi Policy Group [think tank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Think_tank "Think tank").[[241]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-241) For [high altitude](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High_altitude "High altitude")[logistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Logistics "Logistics") at forward outposts, [military robots](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Military_robot "Military robot") are deployed.[[242]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-242)[[243]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-243) Army is developing autonomous combat vehicles, robotic surveillance platforms, and Manned-Unmanned Teaming (MUM-T) solutions as part of the Defence AI roadmap.[[244]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-244)[MCTE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Military_College_of_Telecommunication_Engineering "Military College of Telecommunication Engineering") is working with the [Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology") and, [Society for Applied Microwave Electronics Engineering & Research](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Society_for_Applied_Microwave_Electronics_Engineering_%26_Research "Society for Applied Microwave Electronics Engineering & Research"), on AI and military-grade [chipset](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chipset "Chipset").[[245]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-245)[[246]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-246) Phase III of AI-enabled space-based surveillance has been authorized.[[247]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-247)[[248]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-248)

[DRDO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DRDO "DRDO") Chairman and Secretary of the Department of Defense Research & Development Samir V. Kamat said the agency started concentrating on the potential use of AI in the development of military systems and subsystems.[[249]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-249) The Indian government intends to leverage the private sector's sizable AI workforce and [dual-use technologies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dual-use_technologies "Dual-use technologies") for defense by 2026.[[250]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-250) In order to conduct research on autonomous platforms, improved surveillance, [predictive maintenance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Predictive_maintenance "Predictive maintenance"), and [intelligent decision support system](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intelligent_decision_support_system "Intelligent decision support system"), the Indian Army AI Incubation Center was established.[[251]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-251) Indian Navy launched [INS Surat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INS_Surat "INS Surat") with AI capabilities.[[252]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:03-252)[[253]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-253)

In order to identify and locate any hostile objects in the sky during [Operation Sindoor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operation_Sindoor "Operation Sindoor"), the Indian military employed AI cloud-based integrated air command and control systems.[[254]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-254) The Indian Army tested an AI-powered [Negev NG-7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negev_NG-7 "Negev NG-7") on 9 June 2025, developed by BSS Material.[[255]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-255)[[256]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-256) MoD has approved 129 AI projects until 2026, 77 of which have been finished. To identify and categorize hostile aircraft activities, [BEL](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bharat_Electronics "Bharat Electronics") has created an AI system accessible through the [Integrated Air Command and Control System](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Integrated_Air_Command_and_Control_System "Integrated Air Command and Control System"). To analyze and automate enemy intercepts in the [Western Theater](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Western_Theatre_Command_(India) "Western Theatre Command (India)"), the Indian Army has developed an Intercept Management System that generates intelligence picture by interpreting data using AI and [visualization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_and_information_visualization "Data and information visualization"). It can use AI to perform automated analysis and interpretation, as well as classify and visualize intercepts. To identify airborne targets and eliminate the threat far from susceptible locations, [CAIR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centre_for_Artificial_Intelligence_and_Robotics "Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Robotics") has created an AI-based Air Defense Control and Reporting System.[[257]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-257)

To guarantee smooth data exchange between field units and command centers, [XXXIII Corps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/XXXIII_Corps_(India) "XXXIII Corps (India)") tested AI-enabled sensors with secure communication networks during Exercise Divya Drishti in July 2025. A sensor-to-shooter link was successfully established by the Indian Army, allowing for a quicker and more efficient reaction time.[[258]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-258)[[259]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-259) On 10 September 2025, the Indian Army received a patent for its AI-powered Automatic Target Classifying System. Using real-time data, such as images or radar signals, it can automatically recognize and categorize targets on radar by comparing them to a database of recorded data. It is useful for guiding missiles.[[260]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-260)[[261]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-261)

### Internal security

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Internal security")]

To dismantle [organized crime](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organised_crime_in_India "Organised crime in India") and [terrorism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Terrorism_in_India "Terrorism in India") networks, [Ministry of Home Affairs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Home_Affairs_(India) "Ministry of Home Affairs (India)") established [Organised Crime Network Database](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NATGRID#Organised_Crime_Network_Database "NATGRID") on 26 December 2025. The database was launched by [Union Home Minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Union_Home_Minister "Union Home Minister")[Amit Shah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amit_Shah "Amit Shah") at Anti-Terror Conference in Delhi. Investigative agencies can utilize the OCND, an AI-powered advanced analytics tool to rapidly gather comprehensive information about terrorist networks and [crime syndicates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crime_syndicates "Crime syndicates"). Investigating agencies can instantly access [first information reports](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_information_report "First information report"), [chargesheets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chargesheet "Chargesheet"), dossiers, and other vital information about crimes from all [Indian states](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_states "Indian states"). To create the OCND, the [National Investigation Agency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Investigation_Agency "National Investigation Agency") collaborated closely with state police agencies and [NATGRID](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NATGRID "NATGRID"). OCND functions like [ChatGPT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ChatGPT "ChatGPT") and offers voice matching and fingerprint data in addition to profile data.[[262]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-262)[[263]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-263)

### Space

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Space")]

MOI-TD, India's first AI lab in space, is being built by TakeMe2Space. AI's potential utility in space will be demonstrated with the MOI-TD mission. In order to foresee natural disasters and comprehend climate change, the MOI-TD will assist with environmental monitoring, weather pattern analysis, and Earth's surface change tracking. The lab will process the data in real time, allowing for analysis of the information at the time of collection.[[264]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-264) The MOI-TD mission showed that it was possible to run external code on the satellite, securely downlink encrypted data, and uplink huge AI models from a ground station through OrbitLab, a web-based console. It effectively validated key subsystems, including computer infrastructure (Zero Cube AI Accelerator, [POEM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PSLV_Orbital_Experiment_Module "PSLV Orbital Experiment Module") Adapter Board), actuators (MagnetoTorquers, AirTorquers, [Reaction Wheel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reaction_wheel "Reaction wheel")), and sensors ([Sun Sensor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun_sensor "Sun sensor"), Horizon Sensor, Solar Cell, [IMUs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inertial_measurement_unit "Inertial measurement unit")). The foundation for space-based computing is laid by MOI-TD. The creation of space-based data centers will be facilitated by the upcoming MOI-1 AI Lab mission. Support for the project came from the [Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Center](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_National_Space_Promotion_and_Authorisation_Centre "Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre").[[265]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-265) One study by the [University of Southampton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Southampton "University of Southampton") on TakeMe2Space's AI lab used a low-power AI system to lessen [motion blur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Motion_blur_(media) "Motion blur (media)") in satellite imagery.[[266]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-266)

### Healthcare

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Healthcare")]

During the [COVID-19 pandemic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_India "COVID-19 pandemic in India"), Qure.ai's AI chest X-ray reporting tool, qXR, was used to identify patients for high, medium, or low risk so that [RT-PCR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RT-PCR "RT-PCR") testing could be performed. It can identify [pulmonary consolidation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulmonary_consolidation "Pulmonary consolidation"), [ground-glass opacity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ground-glass_opacity "Ground-glass opacity"), and other signs of [COVID-19](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19"). The tool provides measurements like the proportion of lung size and volume impacted by the anomalies.[[267]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-267)[[268]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-268) The Computational and Data Sciences Collaborative Laboratory of Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Imaging was established by [Wipro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wipro "Wipro")[GE HealthCare](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GE_HealthCare "GE HealthCare") at [IISc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IISc "IISc") in September 2020. Its goal is to develop advanced diagnostic and medical image-reconstruction techniques and protocols for quicker and better imaging by utilizing deep learning, AI, and future-ready digital interfaces.[[269]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-269)[[270]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-270)

With an emphasis on neurological disease diagnosis and population-level clinical impact analysis, [Siemens Healthineers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siemens_Healthineers "Siemens Healthineers") established the Computational Data Sciences Collaborative Laboratory for AI in Precision Medicine at [IISc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IISc "IISc") in 2024 to create [open-source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_source "Open source") AI tools to automate the segmentation of pathological findings in neuroimaging data.[[271]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-271)

As part of the Interdisciplinary Group for Advanced Research on Birth Outcomes – DBT India Initiative (GARBH-Ini) program, [IIT Madras](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Madras "IIT Madras") and [Translational Health Science and Technology Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Translational_Health_Science_and_Technology_Institute "Translational Health Science and Technology Institute") in 2024 created India's first AI model, Garbhini-GA2, which will accurately estimate the age of the [fetus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fetus "Fetus") in [pregnant women](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pregnant_women "Pregnant women") in the [second](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_trimester "Second trimester") and [third trimesters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third_trimester "Third trimester"). In contrast to the current methods of determining a fetus's age in India, which use the Hadlock model developed using pregnancy data from Western countries or the more recent INTERGROWTH-21st model, the Garbhini-GA2, which is specific to the Indian population, uses genetic algorithm-based methods for estimating [gestational age](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gestational_age "Gestational age"). This model reduces the error by nearly three times. The study was carried out in collaboration with [Pondicherry Institute of Medical Sciences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pondicherry_Institute_of_Medical_Sciences "Pondicherry Institute of Medical Sciences"), [Christian Medical College Vellore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christian_Medical_College_Vellore "Christian Medical College Vellore"), [Safdarjung Hospital](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Safdarjung_Hospital "Safdarjung Hospital"), and Gurugram Civil Hospital. Following the completion of pan-India validation, Garbhini-GA2 will be implemented in clinics throughout the country.[[272]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-272)[[273]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-273) The [Ministry of Science and Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Science_and_Technology_(India) "Ministry of Science and Technology (India)")'s [Department of Biotechnology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Biotechnology "Department of Biotechnology") provided financing for the research, with additional support from the Robert Bosch Centre for Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (IIT Madras) and the Centre for Integrative Biology and Systems Medicine (IIT Madras).[[274]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-274)[[275]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-275)

In order to support healthy aging, constructive lifestyle modifications, and psycho-social wellbeing, Wipro and the Centre for Brain Research at IISc partnered in May 2024 to create an AI-based personal care engine that will consider a person's past medical history, desired health state, and behavioral responses to offer personalized help with chronic disease prevention and treatment.[[276]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-276)[[277]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-277) Researchers from [St. John's Hospital](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._John%27s_Medical_College "St. John's Medical College") and [IIIT-B](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIIT-B "IIIT-B") are focusing on using [computer vision](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_vision "Computer vision") for early detection of [autism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autism "Autism").[[278]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-278)

### Mobility

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Mobility")]

In an effort to improve road safety and decrease fatalities by half, [Minister of Road Transport and Highways](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minister_of_Road_Transport_and_Highways "Minister of Road Transport and Highways")[Nitin Gadkari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nitin_Gadkari "Nitin Gadkari") inaugurated Intelligent Solutions for Road Safety through Technology and Engineering (iRASTE) under Mission Zero on 11 September 2021. The pilot project will run for two years in [Nagpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagpur "Nagpur"). [Intel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intel "Intel"), [International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Hyderabad "International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad"), [Central Road Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Road_Research_Institute "Central Road Research Institute"), [Mahindra & Mahindra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahindra_%26_Mahindra "Mahindra & Mahindra"), and [Nagpur Municipal Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagpur_Municipal_Corporation "Nagpur Municipal Corporation") are part of the pilot project. It is focusing on vehicle safety, mobility analysis, and infrastructure safety.[[279]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-279)[[280]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-280)

To improve vehicle safety, [NMC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagpur_Municipal_Corporation "Nagpur Municipal Corporation") will outfit its fleet of vehicles with [advanced driver-assistance systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Advanced_driver-assistance_system "Advanced driver-assistance system") and [collision avoidance systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Collision_avoidance_system "Collision avoidance system"). They will also be equipped with sensors which will continuously monitor the road network's dynamic risks for mobility analysis, mapping the city into three zones: white (normal), grey (accident could happen), and black (accident has already happened). The data will be gathered by Intel AI Center at [IIIT-H](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIIT-H "IIIT-H") for monitoring and fixing the grey and black zones.[[281]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-281)[[282]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-282)

MINRO at IIIT-B is working on traffic analysis of Indian road conditions, analyzing traffic data, and using data for multimodal transport to make recommendations about the locations of [rail transit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Urban_rail_transit_in_India "Urban rail transit in India") and [bus stations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bus_station "Bus station").[[283]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:4-283)

### Government

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Government")]

In 2021, the [Ministry of Corporate Affairs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Corporate_Affairs "Ministry of Corporate Affairs") introduced the first phase of MCA21 V3.0. It will allow for the electronic filing of documents under the [corporate law](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corporate_law "Corporate law") and public access to corporate information. AI will be utilized by the platform to gather, organize, and classify stakeholder comments and inputs and produce analytical reports that will facilitate speedy policy choices.[[284]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-284)

With assistance from [Google](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Google "Google"), the [Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Agriculture_and_Farmers%27_Welfare "Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare") and the [Wadhwani Institute for Artificial Intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wadhwani_Institute_for_Artificial_Intelligence "Wadhwani Institute for Artificial Intelligence") created Krishi 24/7, the first AI-powered automated agricultural news monitoring and analysis tool. Through better decision-making, Krishi 24/7 will support the identification of valuable news, provide timely notifications, and respond quickly to safeguard farmers' interests and advance sustainable agricultural growth. The application converts news articles into English after scanning them in several languages. It ensures that the ministry is informed in a timely manner about pertinent occurrences that are published online by extracting key information from news items, including the headline, crop name, event type, date, location, severity, summary, and source link. The [National Center for Disease Control](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Centre_for_Disease_Control "National Centre for Disease Control") has effectively implemented a comparable automated surveillance and analysis tool for disease outbreaks.[[285]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-285)

As part of the Safe City initiative, 1,000 AI cameras with [facial recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Facial_recognition_system "Facial recognition system") were placed at a cost of ₹96 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore") in [Lucknow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucknow "Lucknow")'s commercial districts, colleges, hostels, large crossings, and public gathering spots. By integrating with the [National Automated Fingerprint Identification System](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Crime_Records_Bureau#National_Automated_Fingerprint_Identification_System "National Crime Records Bureau"), the cameras will detect suspicious movement of individuals by comparing their faces to a police database, notify control rooms of any criminal activities, and send out alerts against [illegal dumping](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Illegal_dumping "Illegal dumping"). For a more responsive and linked security network, AI will be included into [crisis hotlines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crisis_hotline "Crisis hotline") and [helplines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helpline "Helpline").[[286]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-286)

In collaboration with the [Indian government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_government "Indian government"), IIIT-B created Datalake, a tool that analyzes government data on a range of projects to determine whether the objectives of the policy have been fulfilled and whether there is room for improvement. It can forecast how to modify the beneficiary structure to comply with the policy and benefit the citizens.[[283]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:4-283)

The [Government of Uttar Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Uttar_Pradesh "Government of Uttar Pradesh") used [facial recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Facial_recognition_system "Facial recognition system") and AI-powered crowd density monitoring, RFID wristbands as part of the security setup for the [2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_Prayag_Maha_Kumbh_Mela "2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela").[[287]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-287)[[288]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-288) Plans to establish India's first AI lab aimed at strengthening cyber surveillance operations against [deepfake](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deepfake "Deepfake") threats and the country’s first tea auction driven by [blockchain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blockchain "Blockchain") and AI, were announced by the [Assam government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Assam_Government "Assam Government") in the state budget for 2025–2026.[[289]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-289) It will focus on real-time online threat detection, [digital forensics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_forensics "Digital forensics"), and cybersecurity. The lab will track cyberthreats, and assess misleading media.[[290]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-290)

The AI Competency Framework was unveiled on 6 March 2025 by [Ashwini Vaishnaw](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashwini_Vaishnaw "Ashwini Vaishnaw"), the [Minister for Electronics and information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology"), in recognition of the crucial role AI plays in governance. Its goal is to provide public sector professionals with the necessary skills for upskilling programs and AI competency mapping. This framework guarantees informed AI policy-making and implementation by conforming to international best practices. To improve government officials' iGOT Karmayogi platform learning experience, a tailored content suggestion system driven by AI was also unveiled.[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:14-118)[[125]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:15-125)

### Society

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Society")]

[Wadhwani AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wadhwani_Institute_for_Artificial_Intelligence "Wadhwani Institute for Artificial Intelligence"), a $30 million nonprofit research institute, was established in 2018 by [Romesh Wadhwani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romesh_Wadhwani "Romesh Wadhwani") and [Sunil Wadhwani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sunil_Wadhwani "Sunil Wadhwani") with the goal of enhancing the lives of the world's poorest 2 billion people. [New York University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_University "New York University"), the [University of Southern California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Southern_California "University of Southern California"), [India Institutes of Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institutes_of_Technology "Indian Institutes of Technology"), and [University of Mumbai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Mumbai "University of Mumbai") will collaborate with the institute. The objective is to have solutions that show how AI can help the [underprivileged](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Underprivileged "Underprivileged"), and then to build on those initial instances. Wadhwani AI will support universities from [developing nations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Developing_nations "Developing nations") in expanding their AI capabilities.[[291]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-291)

As part of [Microsoft](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft")'s Global Skills for Social Impact charter, the ADVANTA(I)GE India program's first phase trained 2.4 million people in AI skills between 2024 and 2025; 74% of these persons came from [tier 2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tier_2_cities_in_india "Tier 2 cities in india") and [tier 3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classification_of_Indian_cities "Classification of Indian cities") cities, and 65% of them were women. Another 10 million people will be trained by 2030 as part of the second phase.[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:16-75)[[292]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-292)

The [Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology") launched **IndiaAI Application Development Initiative** on 6 March 2025. The aim is to create, scale, and encourage the usage of AI to improve the socioeconomic landscape of the country. In order to solve issues in the fields of healthcare, agriculture, governance, [climate change](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Climate_change_in_India "Climate change in India"), [disaster management](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disaster_management_in_India "Disaster management in India"), and [learning disabilities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Learning_disabilities "Learning disabilities"), IndiaAI has initiated the **IndiaAI****Innovation Challenge**. Enhancing access to public services, improving healthcare, increasing agricultural productivity, helping people with learning disabilities, and lessening the effects of climate change are the goals. With the goal of lowering entry barriers in the field of AI, the **IndiaAI FutureSkills Initiative** will expand the number of AI courses offered in undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral level.
