To deliver foundational level training, IndiaAI Data Labs are being set up in [tier 2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tier_2_cities_in_india "Tier 2 cities in india") and [tier 3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classification_of_Indian_cities "Classification of Indian cities") locations throughout the country. IndiaAI has created the two courses for the positions of Data Curator and Data Annotator, with an emphasis on industries like manufacturing, healthcare, education, and agriculture. These will be taught in IndiaAI Data Labs located throughout 28 centers of the [National Institute of Electronics & Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Institute_of_Electronics_%26_Information_Technology "National Institute of Electronics & Information Technology"), and [industrial training institutes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industrial_training_institute "Industrial training institute").[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:14-118)[[293]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-293)

An MoU was signed on 5 June 2025, between [OpenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAI "OpenAI") and the IndiaAI Mission to establish OpenAI Academy, which would provide AI education training materials in Hindi, English, and four regional languages for students, developers, educators, civil servants, nonprofit leaders, and small business owners.[[294]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-294) It will integrate with IndiaAI FutureSkills Initiative and iGOT Karmayogi platform. One million teachers will receive training on how to use GenAI in the classroom. Additionally, 50 startups or fellows recognized by the IndiaAI Mission will get API credits from OpenAI worth up to $100,000.[[295]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-295) OpenAI has extended its **AI for Impact Accelerator program** by giving 11 [NGOs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NGOs "NGOs") a total of $150,000 in technical funding to develop AI solutions for underserved industries like healthcare, education, and agriculture.[[296]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-296)

### Finance and insurance

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Finance and insurance")]

In India's [banking, financial services and insurance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BFSI "BFSI") (BFSI) industries, AI is already in use as of 2024 and the use of AI by BFSI companies is growing.[[297]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:13-297) Banks in India are using it for [predictive analytics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Predictive_analytics "Predictive analytics"), fraud detection, and personalisation services.[[297]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:13-297) In capital markets, AI is being used for [high-frequency trading](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-frequency_trading "High-frequency trading") and [quantitative analysis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantitative_analysis_(finance) "Quantitative analysis (finance)").[[297]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:13-297) AI is being used by insurance companies for underwriting, fraud detection and damage assessment.[[297]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:13-297) However, with the growth of AI usage in the BFSI sector, the Indian government has also stated that AI, including deepfakes and prompt hacking of large language models, is being used for cyberattacks on BFSI companies.[[298]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-298)

## AI companies of India

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: AI companies of India")]

The following is a list of notable **AI companies of India**, along with their corporate headquarters location.

| Name | Headquarters | AI field | Founded year |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Sarvam AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarvam_AI "Sarvam AI") | [Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengaluru "Bengaluru") | [AI LLM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model") | 2023 |
| [Fractal Analytics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fractal_Analytics "Fractal Analytics") | [New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") | Data analytics | 2000 in Mumbai[[299]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-299) |
| [Glance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glance_(company) "Glance (company)") | [Bengaluru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengaluru "Bengaluru") | Social media | 2019 |
| [Haptik](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Haptik "Haptik") | [Mumbai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai") | Chatbot, intelligence assistant | 2013 in Mumbai[[300]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-300) |
| [Infibeam Avenues Ltd (Phronetic.ai)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infibeam "Infibeam") | [GIFT City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GIFT_City "GIFT City") | Fraud detection and authentication and risk identification in financial & commerce sector | 2010[[301]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-301) |
| [kFin Technologies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karvy_Corporate "Karvy Corporate") | Hyderabad | Insurance | 2017 |
| Ola Krutrim | [Bangalore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore") | [Agentic AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agentic_AI "Agentic AI") and chatbots | 2023 |
| [Niki.ai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Niki.ai "Niki.ai") | Bengaluru | Chatbots | 2015 |
| [Persistent Systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Persistent_Systems "Persistent Systems") | [Pune](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pune "Pune") | Data analytics | 1990 |
| [Tata Technologies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tata_Technologies "Tata Technologies") | Pune | AI/ML-based manufacturing R&D[[302]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-302) | 1989 |
| [Uniphore Software Systems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uniphore "Uniphore") | [Palo Alto, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palo_Alto,_California "Palo Alto, California") | Conversational automation | 2008 in Chennai, India |
| [Yellow.ai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yellow.ai "Yellow.ai") | [San Mateo, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Mateo,_California "San Mateo, California") | Messenger | 2016 in Bangalore, India |
| [Zoho Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zoho_Corporation "Zoho Corporation") | [Chennai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chennai "Chennai") | General AI | 1996 in New Jersey, USA |
| KissanAI | [Surat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surat "Surat") | Agriculture[[303]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-303) | 2023[[304]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-304) |
| [Sarvam AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarvam_AI "Sarvam AI") | Bengaluru | Generative AI, [large language models](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model") | 2023 |
| AAGYAVISION | Bengaluru | Custom-designed chip, radar hardware, and AI-powered algorithms for real-time control and signal analysis[[305]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-305) | 2022[[306]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-306) |
| NxtGen Cloud Technologies | Bengaluru | Agentic and generative AI, sovereign cloud[[307]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-307)[[308]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-308) | 2012 |

## Safety and regulation

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Artificial_intelligence_in_India&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Safety and regulation")]

In order to determine whether to create an [AI Safety Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AI_Safety_Institute "AI Safety Institute") (AISI) that can establish standards, frameworks, and guidelines for AI development without serving as a regulatory body or stifling innovation, [MeitY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeitY "MeitY") conducted consultation process with [Meta Platforms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meta_Platforms "Meta Platforms"), [Google](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Google "Google"), [Microsoft](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft"), [IBM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IBM "IBM"), [OpenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAI "OpenAI"), [NASSCOM](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NASSCOM "NASSCOM"), Broadband India Forum, [Software Alliance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_Alliance "Software Alliance"), [Indian Institutes of Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institutes_of_Technology "Indian Institutes of Technology"), The Quantum Hub, Digital Empowerment Foundation, and [Access Now](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Access_Now "Access Now") on 7 October 2024. It was decided that instead of focusing on regulation, the AISI would focus on damage detection, risk identification, and standards setting for which interoperable systems are necessary to prevent the development of silos which may eventually influence future policies. The Safe and Trusted Pillar of the IndiaAI Mission has been allocated [₹](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E2%82%B9 "₹")20 [crore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crore "Crore"), which the AISI may use for the inaugural budget. More money from the IndiaAI Mission's other verticals may be used in the future.[[309]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-309)[[310]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-310)

In order to develop an AI policy report tailored to India and assess the country's AI ecosystem's strengths and future prospects, [UNESCO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") and MeitY began consulting on AI Readiness Assessment Methodology under Safety and Ethics in Artificial Intelligence from 2024. It is to encourage the ethical and responsible use of AI in industries. The study will help find areas where government can become involved, especially in attempts to strengthen institutional and regulatory capabilities.[[311]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-311)[[312]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-312)

[Minister for Electronics & Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Electronics_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology")[Ashwini Vaishnaw](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashwini_Vaishnaw "Ashwini Vaishnaw") announced the creation of an [IndiaAI Safety Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=IndiaAI_Safety_Institute&action=edit&redlink=1 "IndiaAI Safety Institute (page does not exist)") on 30 January 2025, to ensure the ethical and safe application of AI models. The institute will promote domestic [research and development](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Research_and_development "Research and development") that is grounded in India's social, economic, cultural, and linguistic diversity and is based on Indian datasets. With the help of academic and research institutions, as well as private sector partners, the institute will follow the [hub-and-spoke](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hub-and-spoke "Hub-and-spoke") approach to carry out projects within Safe and Trusted Pillar of the IndiaAI Mission. Among the major safety-related initiatives are eight ongoing projects to implement a techno-legal strategy to protect data privacy while conducting an ethical audit of algorithmic effectiveness.[[119]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:5-119)

First round of projects are as follows:[[313]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-313)

| Theme | Institute | Project |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Machine unlearning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_unlearning "Machine unlearning") | [IIT Jodhpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Jodhpur "IIT Jodhpur") | Develop method for targeted unlearning in [open-source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_source "Open source")[generative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Generative_artificial_intelligence "Generative artificial intelligence")[foundation models](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_model "Foundation model") while minimizing negative impact on overall model performance. |
| [Synthetic data](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Synthetic_data "Synthetic data") generation | [IIT Roorkee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Roorkee "IIT Roorkee") | * Design and develop method for generating synthetic data for mitigating bias in datasets. * Framework for mitigating bias in machine learning pipeline for responsible AI. * Develop algorithm for handling the bias at the model training and in-processing stage of machine learning model development. |
| AI bias mitigation strategy | [National Institute of Technology, Raipur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Institute_of_Technology,_Raipur "National Institute of Technology, Raipur") | Development of algorithms for bias mitigation in health care system applications, image analysis, and diagnostic decisions. |
| [Explainable AI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Explainable_AI "Explainable AI") framework | [Defence Institute of Advanced Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Defence_Institute_of_Advanced_Technology "Defence Institute of Advanced Technology") | * Enabling explainable and privacy preserving AI. * Creating AI models to provide accurate and interpretable results for human activity analysis for effective security in crowded environment. |
| Mindgraph Technologies |
| Privacy enhancing strategies | [IIT Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Delhi "IIT Delhi") | * Privacy preserving machine learning models. * Develop [distributed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distributed_computing "Distributed computing") learning algorithms that can perform in an adversarial environment susceptible to attacks. |
| [IIT Dharwad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIT_Dharwad "IIT Dharwad") |
| [Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indraprastha_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Delhi "Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi") |
| [Telecommunication Engineering Centre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telecommunication_Engineering_Centre "Telecommunication Engineering Centre") |
| AI ethical certification framework | [IIIT-Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IIIT-Delhi "IIIT-Delhi") | * Nishpaksh: tools for assessing fairness of AI model. * Develop three step certification process involving bias risk assessment, processing for metrics, and bias testing to ensure fairness for AI systems in the Indian context. |
| [TEC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telecommunication_Engineering_Centre "Telecommunication Engineering Centre") |
| AI algorithm auditing framework | Civic Data Lab | ParakhAI: open-source framework and toolkit for participatory algorithmic auditing. It will involve citizens in the responsible design, development, and deployment of algorithmic decision making systems. |
| AI governance testing framework | [Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amrita_Vishwa_Vidyapeetham "Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham") | Track [large language model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model"), transparency, risk-assessment, context and knowledge for [LLMs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Large_language_model "Large language model"). Identify and address specific gaps in the existing governance testing frameworks related to LLM's downstream use-case and deployment. |
| TEC |

Themes for second round of projects include [watermarking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Watermark_(data_file) "Watermark (data file)") and labelling, ethical AI frameworks, AI risk-assessment and management, stress testing tools, and [deepfake](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deepfake "Deepfake") detection tools.[[119]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-:5-119)

[Infosys](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infosys "Infosys") released an open-source "Responsible AI" toolbox on 26 February 2025. The program, is a component of the Infosys Topaz Responsible AI Suite. It assists in identifying and stopping security risks, privacy violations, biased outputs, damaging content, copyright infringement, false information, deepfakes, and the use of malevolent AI. The toolkit can improve the transparency of outcomes produced by AI. It works with both on-premises and cloud systems.[[314]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-314) Citing concerns about the confidentiality of government data and documents, the [Ministry of Finance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Finance_(India) "Ministry of Finance (India)") issued an internal department caution advising staff not to use AI tools like [ChatGPT](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ChatGPT "ChatGPT") and [DeepSeek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DeepSeek_(chatbot) "DeepSeek (chatbot)") for work-related purposes.[[315]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-315)

In order to comply with the [Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_Personal_Data_Protection_Act,_2023 "Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023") and other sovereignty requirements, [OpenAI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAI "OpenAI") allowed the local storage of data for ChatGPT Enterprise, ChatGPT Edu, and OpenAI [API](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/API "API") platform customers from 8 May 2025.[[316]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-316)[[317]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-317) Talks to establish data center operations in India have already been started by OpenAI.[[318]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-318)

[Google](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Google "Google") launched its Safety Charter for India's AI-led transformation on 18 June 2025. Keeping end users safe, cybersecurity for government and enterprise infrastructure, and responsibly developing AI are the three core pillars that it outlines for addressing the difficulties of the online world in partnership with the larger ecosystem.[[319]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-319)[[320]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-320) In order to operationalize Google's Safety Charter, Google Safety Engineering Center in [Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabad "Hyderabad") was established.[[321]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence_in_India#cite_note-321)
